title: pondeR • Statistical Analysis Made Easier
text: pondeR R package providing curated collection functions generating publication-ready descriptive inferential statistics tables figures — minimal coding overhead. built upon robust gtsummary framework leverages power tidyverse. package aims make statistical analysis accessible everyone, ensuring transparency analytical methods providing consistent template results.

title: pondeR • Statistical Analysis Made Easier
text: pondeR offers comprehensive suite tools various stages data analysis, particularly focused metabolomics data preprocessing general statistical reporting: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline: Orchestrates sequential preprocessing pipeline metabolomics data combining individual pondeR functions filtering, imputation, batch correction, normalization, transformation, scaling. Summarizing Data & Checking Distributions: Generate publication-ready summary tables, calculate anthropometric indices according standards, test normality skewness. Comparing Groups & Identifying Associations: Provides methods detecting differences cohorts, calculating fold changes, testing statistical associations. Includes plotting functions like volcano plots heatmaps. Predictive Modeling & Classification: Offers tools regularized regression (Ridge, LASSO, Elastic Net), Linear Mixed-Effect Models, Logistic regression analysis performance evaluation (AUC/AUROC) automated bias correction options. Multivariate Exploration Discrimination: Features dimensionality reduction visualization tools (PCA, PLS, PCoA) simplified exploration complex datasets, including scree score plots. Data Cleaning & Quality Control: Functions filtering features based missingness, variance, RSD thresholds. Missing Value Imputation: Addresses missing data using various imputation algorithms. Data Normalization, Transformation, Scaling: Transforms scales data ensure comparability across samples. Signal Drift Batch Effects Correction: Corrects technical variation instrument signal drift. Plotting Functions: variety plotting utilities, including --comparisons, distribution plots, mean maps, multi-Y plots metabolomics metagenomics data. Export Functions: Easily export data frames Excel/CSV files figures PNGs. Utilities & Helpers: collection utility functions data management, relabelling, randomization, retrieving group sizes significant features.

title: pondeR • Statistical Analysis Made Easier
text: can install development version pondeR directly GitHub:
code: # Install devtools if you haven't already if (!requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("devtools") }  devtools::install_github("jllcalorio/pondeR")

title: pondeR • Statistical Analysis Made Easier
text: ’s quick example generate descriptive summary table using pondeR (assuming data frame named my_data): (Note: exact arguments run_summarytable may vary. Please refer package documentation detailed usage.)
code: library(pondeR) library(dplyr) # Often useful with gtsummary-based packages  # Example data (replace with your actual data) my_data <- tibble::tibble(   age = rnorm(100, 30, 5),   gender = sample(c("Male", "Female"), 100, replace = TRUE),   treatment = sample(c("A", "B", "C"), 100, replace = TRUE),   score = runif(100, 0, 100) )  # Generate a summary table my_data %>%   run_summarytable(     # Specify variables and grouping if needed     include = c(age, gender, score),     by = treatment   )

title: Inspect Group Sample Sizes That May Trigger CV Fold Reduction in run_regreg() — get_groupsizes
text: Tabulates number samples per level dependent variable (pred) applying pre-processing steps run_regreg performs: removing rows NA pred, optionally subsetting rows match survive not_penalized NA removal. lets user see exactly run_regreg may warn cv_folds reduced — smallest group fewer samples requested number folds — group bottleneck.

title: Inspect Group Sample Sizes That May Trigger CV Fold Reduction in run_regreg() — get_groupsizes
code: get_groupsizes(   metadata,   pred,   not_penalized = NULL,   is_numeric = NULL,   train_percent = 0.8,   cv_folds = 10L,   show_summary_table = FALSE,   summarytable_args = list() )

title: Inspect Group Sample Sizes That May Trigger CV Fold Reduction in run_regreg() — get_groupsizes
text: metadata data.frame, tibble, named list objects — identical () passed run_regreg(). list, names must set. pred single character string naming dependent variable column metadata. not_penalized Optional character vector. supplied, rows NA metadata-sourced not_penalized column removed counting, mirroring exact sample set run_regreg use. Default: NULL (additional row removal). is_numeric Optional character vector column names listed not_penalized treated numeric, bypassing default factor/character detection. mirrors is_numeric argument run_regreg ensures NA-filtering step uses coercion logic, group sizes counted exactly sample set. example, "Serum albumin (g/L)" stored character truly numeric, listing prevents coerced integer codes — introduce spurious NAs hide real ones. Default: NULL. train_percent numeric scalar (0, 1) matching train_percent argument passed run_regreg(). Used compute projected minimum training-group size. Default: 0.8. cv_folds Integer. cv_folds value passed run_regreg(). Used flag groups whose projected training-set size smaller requested folds. Default: 10L. show_summary_table Logical. TRUE pred categorical, run_summarytable() summary metadata stratified pred printed side-effect. numeric count data frame still returned invisibly. Default: FALSE. summarytable_args named list additional arguments forwarded run_summarytable show_summary_table = TRUE. Default: list().

title: Inspect Group Sample Sizes That May Trigger CV Fold Reduction in run_regreg() — get_groupsizes
text: data.frame following columns: taxon metadata named list. group Level pred variable. n_total Number samples group (NA removal). n_train_projected Projected training-set size: floor(n_total * train_percent). folds_requested value cv_folds. fold_reduction_expected Logical; TRUE n_train_projected < cv_folds. suggested_cv_folds fold count run_regreg actually use: max(3, n_train_projected).

title: Inspect Group Sample Sizes That May Trigger CV Fold Reduction in run_regreg() — get_groupsizes
text: Summarise Group Sizes Relevant Cross-Validation Fold Reduction cross-validation fold reduction warning fires min(table(y_train)) < cv_folds. training set train_percent fraction data, actual minimum group size used fold assignment smaller appears full dataset. function reports full-data group sizes (optionally) projected training-set minimum, user can anticipate reduction running model. optional publication-ready summary table can produced via run_summarytable show_summary_table = TRUE. metadata named list (parallel lapply call), element processed results stacked leading taxon column.

title: Inspect Group Sample Sizes That May Trigger CV Fold Reduction in run_regreg() — get_groupsizes
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Inspect Group Sample Sizes That May Trigger CV Fold Reduction in run_regreg() — get_groupsizes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## ---- Single metadata data frame ------------------------------------------ gs <- get_groupsizes(   metadata      = df_metadata_laboratory |>                     dplyr::filter(`Disease Severity` != "Healthy"),   pred          = "Disease Severity",   not_penalized = vars_include_laboratory,   is_numeric    = "Serum albumin (g/L)",   train_percent = 0.6,   cv_folds      = 10L ) print(gs)  ## ---- With a publication-ready summary table ------------------------------ get_groupsizes(   metadata           = df_metadata_laboratory,   pred               = "Disease Severity",   not_penalized      = vars_include_laboratory,   is_numeric         = "Serum albumin (g/L)",   train_percent      = 0.6,   cv_folds           = 10L,   show_summary_table = TRUE )  ## ---- List of metadata (one per taxon) ------------------------------------ # If each taxon uses the same metadata, just pass it once (not a list). # If each taxon has its own row-subsetted metadata, wrap them in a list: gs_list <- get_groupsizes(   metadata      = setNames(     lapply(names(df_list_clr), function(nm) df_metadata_laboratory),     names(df_list_clr)   ),   pred          = "Disease Severity",   not_penalized = vars_include_laboratory,   train_percent = 0.6,   cv_folds      = 10L ) # Taxa where fold reduction is expected gs_list[gs_list$fold_reduction_expected, ] } # }

what: Get Features with Non-Zero Coefficients from a Regularized Regression Result — get_sigfeatures
title: Get Features with Non-Zero Coefficients from a Regularized Regression Result — get_sigfeatures
text: Extracts names predictor features received non-zero coefficients fitted regularized regression model produced run_regreg. default best-performing model (determined internally run_regreg) used. specific alpha value may requested instead. Intercept terms excluded returned vector unless include_intercept = TRUE. multinomial models, feature may appear multiple group comparisons, union non-zero features across comparisons returned default; set by_comparison = TRUE obtain named list broken comparison.

title: Get Features with Non-Zero Coefficients from a Regularized Regression Result — get_sigfeatures
code: get_sigfeatures(   res,   alpha = NULL,   include_intercept = FALSE,   by_comparison = FALSE,   sort_by = "name",   x_names = NULL )

title: Get Features with Non-Zero Coefficients from a Regularized Regression Result — get_sigfeatures
text: res list class "run_regreg" produced run_regreg. alpha single numeric value [0, 1] specifying fitted model query. Must match one alpha values passed run_regreg. NULL (default), best-performing model selected run_regreg used. include_intercept Logical. TRUE, intercept term(s) ("(Intercept)") retained output. Default: FALSE. by_comparison Logical. relevant multinomial classification results, contain Comparison column coefficient table. TRUE, named list returned element contains non-zero feature names one group comparison (e.g., "B vs "). FALSE (default), union non-zero features across comparisons returned single character vector. Ignored binary classification regression results. sort_by single string controlling returned feature names ordered. One : "name" Alphabetical order (default). "coef" Descending absolute coefficient magnitude. available by_comparison = TRUE result multinomial (union step discards magnitudes); warning issued function falls back "name" case. "none" Preserve original row order coefficient table. x_names optional character vector column names original x passed run_regreg(). supplied, features whose names appear x_names returned, silently dropping unpenalized metadata columns appended via not_penalized. NULL (default), function first checks whether res stores original x column names internally (via res$x_names); found, used automatically. neither available, name-based filtering applied note issued. simplest usage x_names = colnames(x).

title: Get Features with Non-Zero Coefficients from a Regularized Regression Result — get_sigfeatures
text: Default (by_comparison = FALSE) character vector feature names non-zero coefficients, order specified sort_by. Returns character(0) message non-zero features found (e.g., null model). by_comparison = TRUE (multinomial ) named list character vectors, one per group comparison. element named comparison label (e.g., "B vs ").

title: Get Features with Non-Zero Coefficients from a Regularized Regression Result — get_sigfeatures
text: Extract Significant Features pondeR Model Result

title: Get Features with Non-Zero Coefficients from a Regularized Regression Result — get_sigfeatures
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## ---- Setup --------------------------------------------------------------- set.seed(42) n  <- 80 p  <- 30  feat <- as.data.frame(   matrix(rnorm(n * p), nrow = n,          dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("feat_", seq_len(p)))) ) meta <- data.frame(   Group = sample(c("Control", "Case"), n, replace = TRUE) )  ## ---- Binary classification ----------------------------------------------- res_bin <- run_regreg(   x    = feat, metadata = meta,   pred = "Group", ref = "Control",   alpha = c(0, 0.5, 1), seed = 123 )  # Best model features (default) get_sigfeatures(res_bin)  # Features from LASSO specifically get_sigfeatures(res_bin, alpha = 1)  # Sorted by absolute coefficient magnitude get_sigfeatures(res_bin, sort_by = "coef")  # Include the intercept term get_sigfeatures(res_bin, include_intercept = TRUE)  ## ---- Multinomial classification ------------------------------------------ meta3 <- data.frame(   Group = sample(c("A", "B", "C"), n, replace = TRUE) ) res_multi <- run_regreg(   x    = feat, metadata = meta3,   pred = "Group", ref = "A",   alpha = 0.5, seed = 1 )  # Union of non-zero features across all comparisons get_sigfeatures(res_multi)  # Per-comparison breakdown get_sigfeatures(res_multi, by_comparison = TRUE)  ## ---- Gaussian regression ------------------------------------------------- meta_num <- data.frame(Score = rnorm(n)) res_num  <- run_regreg(   x    = feat, metadata = meta_num,   pred = "Score", alpha = 0.5, seed = 7 ) get_sigfeatures(res_num) } # }

code: has_inf(x, warn = TRUE)

title: Check if an Object Contains Infinite Values — has_inf
text: x R object test. warn Logical scalar. TRUE (default), warning emitted x NULL unsupported type.

title: Check if an Object Contains Infinite Values — has_inf
text: TRUE least one Inf -Inf found, FALSE otherwise. Never NA.

title: Check if an Object Contains Infinite Values — has_inf
text: positive (Inf) negative (-Inf) infinite values trigger TRUE result. NaN NA infinite (consistent .infinite() base R). tabular inputs entire table scanned; vectors list inputs elements scanned unlisting.

title: Check if an Object Contains Infinite Values — has_inf
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Check if an Object Contains Infinite Values — has_inf
code: has_inf(c(1, Inf, 3))           # TRUE #> [1] TRUE has_inf(c(1, -Inf, 3))          # TRUE #> [1] TRUE has_inf(c(1, NaN, NA))          # FALSE - NaN/NA are not Inf #> [1] FALSE has_inf(c(1, 2, 3))             # FALSE #> [1] FALSE  df <- data.frame(x = c(1, Inf), y = c(3, 4)) has_inf(df)                      # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  mat <- matrix(c(1, 2, -Inf, 4), nrow = 2) has_inf(mat)                     # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

what: Check if an Object has Names — has_names
title: Check if an Object has Names — has_names
text: Tests whether object names. tabular objects (data.frame, tibble, data.table, matrix), column names checked. plain lists, element names checked. atomic vectors, element names checked. Returns single logical value (TRUE FALSE).

title: Check if an Object has Names — has_names
code: has_names(x, warn = TRUE)

title: Check if an Object has Names — has_names
text: x R object test. Supported types: data.frame, tibble, data.table, matrix, plain list, atomic vector. warn Logical scalar. TRUE (default), informative warnings emitted describing x fails check.

title: Check if an Object has Names — has_names
text: single logical value: TRUE names present non-empty, FALSE otherwise. Never NA.

title: Check if an Object has Names — has_names
text: object considered named names() (colnames() tabular objects) returns non-NULL character vector every element non-empty, non-NA string. partially named object (names "" NA) returns FALSE, warning emitted identifying problematic positions. NULL always returns FALSE. Unsupported object types (e.g., environments, S4 objects) also return FALSE warning.

title: Check if an Object has Names — has_names
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Check if an Object has Names — has_names
code: # Tabular - TRUE has_names(mtcars) #> [1] TRUE  # Matrix - TRUE has_names(matrix(1:4, 2, dimnames = list(NULL, c("A", "B")))) #> [1] TRUE  # Matrix without column names - FALSE has_names(matrix(1:4, 2)) #> Warning: 'x' has no column names attribute. #> [1] FALSE  # Named list - TRUE has_names(list(a = 1, b = 2)) #> [1] TRUE  # Unnamed list - FALSE has_names(list(1, 2, 3)) #> Warning: 'x' has no names attribute. #> [1] FALSE  # Named vector - TRUE has_names(c(x = 1, y = 2)) #> [1] TRUE  # Partially named list - FALSE has_names(list(a = 1, 2, b = 3)) #> Warning: 'x' has missing or empty names at position(s): 2. #> [1] FALSE  # NULL - FALSE has_names(NULL) #> Warning: 'x' is NULL. #> [1] FALSE

what: Check if an Object is a Plain List — is_list
title: Check if an Object is a Plain List — is_list
text: Tests whether object base R list (data.frame, tibble, data.table, technically lists hood). Returns single logical value (TRUE FALSE).

title: Check if an Object is a Plain List — is_list
code: is_list(x, warn = TRUE)

title: Check if an Object is a Plain List — is_list
text: x R object test. warn Logical scalar. TRUE (default), informative warning emitted x fails check. Set FALSE suppress warnings obtain silent FALSE.

title: Check if an Object is a Plain List — is_list
text: single logical value: TRUE x plain list, FALSE otherwise. Never NA.

title: Check if an Object is a Plain List — is_list
text: is_list() returns TRUE x plain list - .e., .list(x) TRUE x inherit tabular class (data.frame, tbl_df, tbl, data.table, matrix). distinction matters data.frame data.table objects internally lists treated context pondeR workflows. NULL always returns FALSE.

title: Check if an Object is a Plain List — is_list
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Check if an Object is a Plain List — is_list
code: # TRUE cases is_list(list(a = 1, b = "hello")) #> [1] TRUE is_list(list(1:3, TRUE, NULL)) #> [1] TRUE  # FALSE cases - tabular objects are excluded is_list(mtcars) #> Warning: 'x' has class <data.frame>, which is a tabular object, not a plain list. Use is_tabular() to check for data.frame, tibble, data.table, or matrix objects. #> [1] FALSE is_list(matrix(1:4, 2)) #> Warning: 'x' has class <matrix, array>, which is a tabular object, not a plain list. Use is_tabular() to check for data.frame, tibble, data.table, or matrix objects. #> [1] FALSE  # FALSE - atomic vector is_list(1:10) #> Warning: 'x' has class <integer>, which is not a list. A plain list object was expected. #> [1] FALSE  # FALSE - NULL is_list(NULL) #> Warning: 'x' is NULL. A plain list was expected. #> [1] FALSE  # Silent FALSE for programmatic use is_list(42, warn = FALSE) #> [1] FALSE

what: Check if an Object is a Tabular Data Structure — is_tabular
title: Check if an Object is a Tabular Data Structure — is_tabular
text: Tests whether object belongs recognized tabular data class - data.frame, tbl_df (tibble), data.table, matrix - confirms exactly two dimensions. Returns single logical value (TRUE FALSE).

title: Check if an Object is a Tabular Data Structure — is_tabular
code: is_tabular(x, warn = TRUE)

title: Check if an Object is a Tabular Data Structure — is_tabular
text: x R object test. warn Logical scalar. TRUE (default), informative warnings emitted x fails check, describing specific reason failure. Set FALSE suppress warnings obtain silent FALSE.

title: Check if an Object is a Tabular Data Structure — is_tabular
text: single logical value: TRUE x recognized tabular structure two dimensions, FALSE otherwise. return value never NA.

title: Check if an Object is a Tabular Data Structure — is_tabular
text: object considered tabular satisfies following conditions: class one "data.frame", "tbl_df", "tbl" (generic tibble superclass), "data.table", "matrix". exactly two dimensions (.e., length(dim(x)) == 2). class check uses inherits(), subclasses data.frame (e.g., tibbles, inherit "data.frame") naturally detected. matrix always two dimensions construction, dimension check applied uniformly consistency guard unusual edge cases zero-dimensional matrices produced certain external packages. NULL objects class attribute handled gracefully: always return FALSE. Note is_tabular() check whether object non-empty. zero-row zero-column tabular object still return TRUE, retains correct class dimensions. Callers require non-empty data perform check separately.

title: Check if an Object is a Tabular Data Structure — is_tabular
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Check if an Object is a Tabular Data Structure — is_tabular
code: # --- TRUE cases ---  # Base data.frame is_tabular(mtcars) #> [1] TRUE  # Matrix is_tabular(matrix(1:12, nrow = 3)) #> [1] TRUE  # tibble (if tibble is installed) if (requireNamespace("tibble", quietly = TRUE)) {   is_tabular(tibble::tibble(x = 1:3, y = letters[1:3])) } #> [1] TRUE  # data.table (if data.table is installed) if (requireNamespace("data.table", quietly = TRUE)) {   is_tabular(data.table::data.table(a = 1:5, b = rnorm(5))) } #> [1] TRUE  # Zero-row data.frame - still tabular is_tabular(mtcars[0, ]) #> [1] TRUE  # --- FALSE cases ---  # Atomic vector is_tabular(1:10) #> Warning: 'x' has class <integer>, which is not a recognized tabular type. Expected one of: data.frame, tibble (tbl_df), data.table, or matrix. #> [1] FALSE  # List is_tabular(list(a = 1, b = 2)) #> Warning: 'x' has class <list>, which is not a recognized tabular type. Expected one of: data.frame, tibble (tbl_df), data.table, or matrix. #> [1] FALSE  # 3-D array (more than two dimensions) is_tabular(array(1:24, dim = c(2, 3, 4))) #> Warning: 'x' has class <array>, which is not a recognized tabular type. Expected one of: data.frame, tibble (tbl_df), data.table, or matrix. #> [1] FALSE  # NULL is_tabular(NULL) #> Warning: 'x' is NULL. A tabular object (data.frame, tibble, data.table, or matrix) was expected. #> [1] FALSE  # Suppress warnings for programmatic use is_tabular("not a table", warn = FALSE) #> [1] FALSE

what: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: Generates multi-panel plot comparing signal selected features data transformation, correction, manipulation. panel displays one feature across injection sequence, colored sample group shaped batch, optional LOESS trend line fitted user-defined reference group (e.g., QC samples).

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
code: plot_beforeafter(   x,   y,   metadata,   plot_what = NULL,   col_injection = "InjectionSequence",   col_batch = "Batch",   col_group = "Group",   col_qc_label = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   x_label = "Before",   y_label = "After",   log_transform = TRUE,   point_size = 2,   point_alpha = 0.7,   plot_cols = NULL,   theme = "nature",   base_size = 11,   font_family = "sans",   seed = 123,   global_font_size = NULL,   title_size = NULL,   subtitle_size = NULL,   xlab_size = NULL,   ylab_size = NULL )

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: x data.frame, tibble, matrix representing data transformation. Rows samples; columns features. Column names may contain special characters. Input can link{run_DIpreprocess} class. y data.frame, tibble, matrix representing data transformation. Must dimensions column names x. metadata data.frame containing sample-level metadata shared x y. Must number rows x y. plot_what Character vector NULL. Column names x plotted. NULL (default), function randomly select 6 feature names x. x fewer 6 features, available features plotted. argument required. col_injection Character. Name column metadata containing injection sequence (numeric order runs). Default "InjectionSequence". run_DIpreprocess class, value automatically taken $parameters$injection_col. col_batch Character. Name column metadata containing batch identifiers. Default "Batch". run_DIpreprocess class, value automatically taken $parameters$batch_col. col_group Character. Name column metadata containing sample group labels (e.g., "QC", "Sample"). Default "Group". run_DIpreprocess class, value automatically taken $parameters$group_col. col_qc_label Character vector. value(s) col_group column identifies reference group LOESS trend fitting (typically QC samples). Can vector like c("SQC", "EQC"). NULL, LOESS line drawn. Default c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). run_DIpreprocess class, value automatically taken $parameters$qc_types. x_label Character. Label -correction facet panel. Default "". y_label Character. Label -correction facet panel. Default "". log_transform Logical. TRUE, applies log10 transformation (small floor 1e-10) feature values plotting. Default TRUE. point_size Numeric. Size individual data points. Default 2. point_alpha Numeric [0, 1]. Transparency data points. Default 0.7. plot_cols Integer NULL. Number columns combined plot grid. NULL, determined automatically. Default NULL. theme Character. ggplot2 theme apply panel. Options: "nature", "minimal", "classic", "bw", "light", "dark". Default "nature" (clean, publication-ready style based theme_bw()). base_size Numeric. Base font size theme (pts). Default 11. font_family Character. Font family text elements. Default "sans". seed Numeric NULL. Random seed passed set.seed() internal sampling operations. Default 123. global_font_size Numeric NULL. Base font size proportional scaling text elements. Default NULL. title_size Numeric NULL. Feature panel title font size. Default NULL. subtitle_size Numeric NULL. Main plot title (top grob) font size. Default NULL. xlab_size Numeric NULL. X-axis label font size. Default NULL. ylab_size Numeric NULL. Y-axis label font size. Default NULL.

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: Invisibly returns gtable grob object (gridExtra::arrangeGrob()) containing feature panels. plot also drawn active graphics device.

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: Plot Pairwise --Comparison Selected Features x y must share identical row counts, column counts, column names. Row order assumed correspond x, y, metadata. log_transform = TRUE, values floored 1e-10 log transformation avoid -Inf results zero negative values. Font sizes resolved following priority (highest lowest): explicit size argument (e.g., title_size) > global_font_size scaling > base_size-derived default. global_font_size provided specific size , text elements scale proportionally relative base size 11. LOESS trend line fitted group identified col_qc_label. fewer 3 observations group present, warning issued line suppressed feature. combined plot rendered via gridExtra::arrangeGrob() displayed grid::grid.draw(). returned object invisible gtable grob can redrawn exported ggplot2::ggsave().

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) n_samples  <- 30 n_features <- 4  x <- as.data.frame(   matrix(abs(rnorm(n_samples * n_features, mean = 1000, sd = 200)),          nrow = n_samples,          dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("Feature_", seq_len(n_features)))) ) y <- as.data.frame(x + rnorm(n_samples * n_features, sd = 50))  meta <- data.frame(   InjectionSequence = seq_len(n_samples),   Batch             = rep(c("B1", "B2"), each = n_samples / 2),   Group             = rep(c("Sample", "QC"), times = c(n_samples - 6, 6)) )  plot_beforeafter(   x             = x,   y             = y,   metadata      = meta,   plot_what     = c("Feature_1", "Feature_2"),   col_injection = "InjectionSequence",   col_batch     = "Batch",   col_group     = "Group",   col_qc_label  = "QC",   theme         = "nature" ) } # }

what: Plot a Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_dend
title: Plot a Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_dend
text: Produces publication-ready dendrogram data frame, tibble, matrix using hierarchical clustering. Supports row-wise column-wise clustering configurable distance metrics agglomeration methods. Returns ggplot2 object via ggdendro.

title: Plot a Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_dend
code: plot_dend(   x,   clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",   clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",   agglomeration_method = "ward.D",   orientation = "rows",   theme = "nature",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   global_font_size = 15,   axis_title_size = NULL,   axis_text_size = NULL,   legend_title_size = NULL,   legend_text_size = NULL,   plot_title_size = NULL,   plot_subtitle_size = NULL,   label_size = NULL,   line_width = 0.5,   line_color = "black",   ... )

title: Plot a Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_dend
text: x data frame, tibble, matrix numeric values cluster. Column names may contain special characters. clustering_distance_rows single character string specifying distance metric row clustering. One "euclidean" (default), "maximum", "manhattan", "canberra", "binary", "minkowski". See dist. clustering_distance_cols single character string specifying distance metric column clustering. options clustering_distance_rows. Default "euclidean". agglomeration_method single character string specifying agglomeration method passed hclust. One "ward.D" (default), "ward.D2", "single", "complete", "average", "mcquitty", "median", "centroid". orientation single character string. One "rows" (default; clusters rows), "cols" (clusters columns), "" (returns named list two ggplot2 objects). theme single character string specifying plot theme. One "nature" (default), "classic", "minimal", "bw". title single character string plot title. Default NULL (title). subtitle single character string plot subtitle. Default NULL (subtitle). global_font_size single positive numeric specifying base font size points. Default 15. axis_title_size single positive numeric axis title font size. Defaults global_font_size. axis_text_size single positive numeric axis text font size. Defaults global_font_size. legend_title_size single positive numeric legend title font size. Defaults global_font_size. legend_text_size single positive numeric legend text font size. Defaults global_font_size. plot_title_size single positive numeric plot title font size. Defaults global_font_size + 2. plot_subtitle_size single positive numeric plot subtitle font size. Defaults global_font_size. label_size single positive numeric controlling font size leaf labels. Defaults global_font_size * 0.8. line_width single positive numeric dendrogram branch width. Default 0.5. line_color single character string branch colour. Default "black". ... Additional arguments passed dist hclust.

title: Plot a Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_dend
text: ggplot2 object, named list two ggplot2 objects (rows cols) orientation = "".

title: Plot a Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_dend
text: Numeric columns extracted scaled (zero mean, unit variance) computing distances. Rows columns containing NA values silently dropped. Remaining NAs imputed column means scaling. orientation = "", returned object named list elements rows cols.

title: Plot a Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_dend
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Plot a Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_dend
code: ## Row dendrogram (default) plot_dend(mtcars)   ## Column dendrogram with Manhattan distance plot_dend(mtcars, orientation = "cols",           clustering_distance_cols = "manhattan")   ## Both axes dends <- plot_dend(mtcars, orientation = "both") dends$rows  dends$cols   ## Custom styling plot_dend(mtcars, theme = "minimal", line_color = "#2166AC",           line_width = 0.8, global_font_size = 10,           title = "Sample Clustering")

what: Create Box or Violin Plots with Statistical Comparisons — plot_diff
title: Create Box or Violin Plots with Statistical Comparisons — plot_diff
text: Generates box plots violin plots statistical tests pairwise comparisons. Automatically selects appropriate statistical tests based assumptions, allows manual specification. Statistical testing performed internally via .run_diff_single, unexported single-outcome engine powers run_diff.

title: Create Box or Violin Plots with Statistical Comparisons — plot_diff
code: plot_diff(   x,   outcome,   group,   plot_vars = NULL,   plot_type = "boxplot",   test_type = NULL,   posthoc = NULL,   p_adjust_method = "BH",   hide_ns = TRUE,   show_p_numeric = FALSE,   paired = FALSE,   test_alpha = 0.05,   colors = NULL,   plot_title = NULL,   plot_subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   linewidth = 3,   show_global_p = FALSE,   global_p_position = NULL,   global_p_x = NULL,   global_font_size = 30,   title_size = NULL,   subtitle_size = NULL,   xlab_size = NULL,   ylab_size = NULL,   pvalue_size = NULL,   axis_text_size = NULL,   format_p_numeric = "auto",   group_order = NULL,   label_points = NULL,   sample_id = NULL,   show_mean = FALSE,   show_agg_val = TRUE,   horizontal = FALSE,   bracket_spacing = 0.08,   subgroup = NULL,   ... )

title: Create Box or Violin Plots with Statistical Comparisons — plot_diff
text: x data frame containing variables plot. outcome Character string specifying column name numeric outcome variable. Used default plot_vars specified. group Character string specifying column name grouping variable. plot_vars Character vector specifying column name(s) numeric variables plot. NULL (default), uses value outcome. plot_type Character string specifying plot type: "boxplot" "violin". Default "boxplot". test_type Character string NULL. Statistical test strategy: "auto", "parametric", "nonparametric". See run_diff details. NULL (default), uses "auto". posthoc Character string NULL. Retained API compatibility; post-hoc selection handled automatically internal engine. Default NULL. p_adjust_method Character string. P-value adjustment method post-hoc comparisons: "holm", "hochberg", "hommel", "bonferroni", "BH", "", "fdr", "none". Default "BH". hide_ns Logical. TRUE, hide non-significant p-values plot brackets. Default TRUE. show_p_numeric Logical. TRUE, show exact p-values instead significance symbols. Default FALSE. paired Logical. TRUE, use paired tests. Default FALSE. test_alpha Numeric. Significance level determining significance plot. Default 0.05. colors Character vector colors NULL automatic soft colors. Default NULL. plot_title Character vector NULL. Custom plot title(s). vector, match length variables plotted. Default NULL. plot_subtitle Character string NULL. Custom subtitle. NULL, test name shown. Default NULL. xlab Character string NULL. X-axis label. Default NULL. ylab Character string NULL. Y-axis label. Default NULL. linewidth Numeric. width lines box/violin outlines statistical brackets. Default 3. show_global_p Logical. TRUE, annotates plot omnibus p-value. Default FALSE. global_p_position Numeric NULL. Y-axis position global p-value annotation. Default NULL (automatic). global_p_x Numeric NULL. X-axis position global p-value annotation. Default NULL (automatic). global_font_size Numeric NULL. Base font size proportional scaling text elements. Default 30. title_size Numeric NULL. Title font size. Default NULL. subtitle_size Numeric NULL. Subtitle font size. Default NULL. xlab_size Numeric NULL. X-axis label font size. Default NULL. ylab_size Numeric NULL. Y-axis label font size. Default NULL. pvalue_size Numeric NULL. Font size p-value annotations (ggplot2 size units). Default NULL. axis_text_size Numeric NULL. Axis tick label font size. Default NULL. format_p_numeric Character string numeric. Controls p-value formatting show_p_numeric = TRUE. "auto" (default) uses 3 decimal places. group_order Character vector NULL. Group order x-axis. Default NULL. label_points Character string NULL. "" labels jitter points; "outliers" labels outliers. Requires sample_id. Default NULL. sample_id Character string NULL. Column name sample identifiers used point labeling. Default NULL. show_mean Logical. TRUE, adds red dot (mean indicator) group. Default FALSE. show_agg_val Logical. TRUE (default), shows numeric mean (parametric) median (non-parametric) value text plot. horizontal Logical. TRUE, flips horizontal orientation. Default FALSE. bracket_spacing Numeric. Multiplier vertical spacing comparison brackets. Default 0.08. subgroup Character vector NULL. Column name(s) subgroup analysis. Default NULL. ... Additional arguments passed ggboxplot ggviolin ggpubr.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example 1: Box plot with mean dot and mean value annotation results <- plot_diff(   x            = PlantGrowth,   outcome      = "weight",   group        = "group",   show_mean    = TRUE,   show_agg_val = TRUE ) print(results$plots$weight)  # Example 2: Global font size scaling and omnibus p-value annotation results2 <- plot_diff(   x                = PlantGrowth,   outcome          = "weight",   group            = "group",   global_font_size = 16,   show_global_p    = TRUE ) print(results2$plots$weight)  # Example 3: Paired comparison with numeric p-values sleep_data    <- sleep sleep_data$ID <- rep(1:10, 2) results3 <- plot_diff(   x                = sleep_data,   outcome          = "extra",   group            = "group",   paired           = TRUE,   show_p_numeric   = TRUE,   format_p_numeric = 4 ) print(results3$plots$extra) } # }

what: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: Generates density box plots comparing data distributions transformation, normalization, scaling, correction step. Plots can oriented sample feature. Returns named list individual ggplot2 objects combined gtable grob.

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
code: plot_dist_beforeafter(   x,   y,   group_by = "sample",   plot_what = NULL,   n_random = 30L,   x_label = "Before",   y_label = "After",   x_fill = "#56B4E9",   y_fill = "#E69F00",   point_alpha = 0.6,   theme = "nature",   base_size = 11,   font_family = "sans",   plot_cols = NULL,   seed = 123,   global_font_size = NULL,   title_size = NULL,   subtitle_size = NULL,   xlab_size = NULL,   ylab_size = NULL )

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: x data.frame, tibble, matrix representing data transformation. Rows samples; columns features. Column names may contain special characters. y data.frame, tibble, matrix representing data transformation. Must dimensions column names x. group_by Character. Perspective box plots. "sample" shows one box per sample; "feature" shows one box per feature. Default "sample". plot_what Character vector NULL. Subset column names (features) x y include plots. NULL, features used. Default NULL. n_random Integer NULL. Maximum number samples (group_by = "sample") features (group_by =   "feature") display box plots. NULL data fewer items n_random, shown. Default 30. x_label Character. Label describing state, used plot titles. Default "". y_label Character. Label describing state, used plot titles. Default "". x_fill Character. Fill color density box plots. Default "#56B4E9" (Okabe-Ito sky blue). y_fill Character. Fill color density box plots. Default "#E69F00" (Okabe-Ito orange). point_alpha Numeric [0, 1]. Transparency density fill box fill. Default 0.6. theme Character. ggplot2 theme apply. Options: "nature", "minimal", "classic", "bw", "light", "dark". Default "nature". base_size Numeric. Base font size theme (pts). Default 11. font_family Character. Font family text elements. Default "sans". plot_cols Integer NULL. Number columns combined output grid. NULL, defaults 2 (density row + boxplot row). Default NULL. seed Numeric NULL. Random seed reproducible sampling n_random used. Default 123. global_font_size Numeric NULL. Base font size proportional scaling text elements. Default NULL. title_size Numeric NULL. Plot panel title font size. Default NULL. subtitle_size Numeric NULL. Combined plot main title font size. Default NULL. xlab_size Numeric NULL. X-axis label font size. Default NULL. ylab_size Numeric NULL. Y-axis label font size. Default NULL.

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: named list containing: plot_density_before Density plot x. plot_density_after Density plot y. plot_box_before Box plot x. plot_box_after Box plot y. plot_combined gtable grob four panels, drawn active device.

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: Plot Distribution Comparison Data Transformation x y must share identical row counts, column counts, column names. Row order assumed correspond x y. plot_what specified, features included density box plots. n_random also specified, random sub-sampling applied top plot_what subset box plots ; density plots always use full plot_what subset. Box plot items ordered median value readability. Font sizes follow resolution priority plot_beforeafter: explicit argument > global_font_size scaling > base_size derived default. function renders combined plot active graphics device also returns named list individual panels can extracted, re-styled, exported independently.

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) n_samples  <- 40 n_features <- 20  x <- as.data.frame(   matrix(abs(rnorm(n_samples * n_features, mean = 1000, sd = 300)),          nrow = n_samples,          dimnames = list(            paste0("S", seq_len(n_samples)),            paste0("Feature_", seq_len(n_features))          )) ) y <- as.data.frame(scale(x))  # Sample-wise comparison, default settings result <- plot_dist_beforeafter(   x       = x,   y       = y,   group_by = "sample" )  # Feature-wise, subset of features, classic theme result <- plot_dist_beforeafter(   x         = x,   y         = y,   group_by  = "feature",   plot_what = paste0("Feature_", 1:10),   theme     = "classic" )  # Extract individual panels result$plot_density_before result$plot_box_after } # }

title: Plot a Correlation Heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: Produces publication-ready correlation heatmap optional hierarchical clustering dendrograms, significance masking, triangle display, -cell coefficient annotations. Internally calls run_correl returns ggplot2 object.

title: Plot a Correlation Heatmap — plot_heatmap
code: plot_heatmap(   x,   method = "auto",   out = "all",   sig_threshold = 0.05,   remove = NULL,   theme = "nature",   plot_title = NULL,   plot_subtitle = NULL,   show_rlab = TRUE,   show_clab = TRUE,   top_n = NULL,   bottom_n = NULL,   color = c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B"),   clab_angle = 45,   show_triangle = "all",   show_coef = FALSE,   round = 2L,   mark_x = TRUE,   mark_x_size = 1,   mark_x_alpha = 0.6,   show_dend = FALSE,   dend_row_width = 0.2,   dend_col_height = 0.2,   global_font_size = 15,   axis_title_size = NULL,   axis_text_size = NULL,   legend_title_size = NULL,   legend_text_size = NULL,   coef_text_size = NULL,   plot_title_size = NULL,   plot_subtitle_size = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot a Correlation Heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: x data frame, tibble, matrix. Column names may contain special characters. method Correlation method passed run_correl. One "auto" (default), "pearson", "spearman", "kendall", "pointbiserial", "phi", "cramerv", "poly". single character string: "" (default; plot correlations) "masked" (set non-significant cells NA). sig_threshold Significance threshold passed run_correl. Default 0.05. remove Level-exclusion list passed run_correl. theme single character string: "nature" (default), "classic", "minimal", "bw". plot_title single character string plot title. Default NULL. plot_subtitle single character string plot subtitle. Default NULL. show_rlab Logical. Show row labels. Default TRUE. show_clab Logical. Show column labels. Default TRUE. top_n single positive integer. Plot top_n pairs highest absolute correlation. Mutually exclusive bottom_n. Default NULL. bottom_n single positive integer. Plot bottom_n pairs lowest absolute correlation. Mutually exclusive top_n. Default NULL. color character vector length 3 specifying fill colours negative, neutral, positive correlation respectively. Default c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B"). clab_angle single numeric (0–360) controlling rotation angle column (x-axis) labels degrees. Default 45. show_triangle single character string: "" (default), "lower" (retain lower triangle ), "upper" (retain upper triangle ). show_coef Logical. Annotate cell rounded correlation coefficient. Default FALSE. round single non-negative integer. Number decimal places coefficient annotation. Default 2. rounding produces exactly 1 -1 non-unity value, scientific notation used font reduced. Ignored show_coef = FALSE. mark_x Logical. Overlay "\u00D7" non-significant cells show_coef = TRUE. Default TRUE. mark_x_size single positive numeric acting multiplier size "×" mark. Default 1.0 (fills ~85% cell). Ignored mark_x = FALSE. mark_x_alpha single numeric (0–1) controlling transparency "×" mark. Default 0.6. Ignored mark_x = FALSE. show_dend Logical. Whether attach hierarchical clustering dendrograms heatmap margins. Requires patchwork. Default FALSE. dend_row_width single positive numeric controlling relative width row dendrogram panel show_dend = TRUE. Default 0.2. dend_col_height single positive numeric controlling relative height column dendrogram panel show_dend = TRUE. Default 0.2. global_font_size single positive numeric specifying base font size points. Default 15. axis_title_size Font size axis titles. Defaults global_font_size. axis_text_size Font size axis tick labels. Defaults global_font_size. legend_title_size Font size legend title. Defaults global_font_size. legend_text_size Font size legend text. Defaults global_font_size. coef_text_size Font size -cell coefficient annotations. Defaults global_font_size * 0.7. Ignored show_coef = FALSE. plot_title_size Font size plot title. Defaults global_font_size + 2. plot_subtitle_size Font size plot subtitle. Defaults global_font_size. ... Additional arguments passed run_correl , show_dend = TRUE, plot_dend.

Arguments accepted plot_dend (e.g. agglomeration_method, line_color) forwarded automatically via ....

what: Mean Intensity Heatmap with Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_meanmap
title: Mean Intensity Heatmap with Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_meanmap
text: Produces publication-ready mean intensity heatmap rows variables (e.g. metabolites) clustered hierarchical clustering columns group means derived grouping variable metadata. coloured dendrogram aligned left heatmap, optional cluster bracket annotations per-row significance markers right. function intended visualising group-level mean differences across variables — example, metabolite intensities across disease stages — distinct plot_clustermap, visualises variable--variable correlation structure.

title: Mean Intensity Heatmap with Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_meanmap
code: plot_meanmap(   x,   metadata = NULL,   group,   group_order = NULL,   sig_vars = NULL,   sig_marker = "*",   sig_color = "red",   clust_method = "ward.D2",   dist_method = "euclidean",   scale_vars = "none",   n_clusters = NULL,   cluster_labels = NULL,   cluster_colors = NULL,   heatmap_colors = c("#008000", "#FFFF00", "#FF0000"),   show_values = FALSE,   value_digits = 2L,   value_color = "black",   global_font_size = 11,   row_font_size = NULL,   col_font_size = NULL,   legend_font_size = NULL,   cluster_label_size = NULL,   sig_marker_size = NULL,   dend_width = 0.2,   legend_position = "right",   na_color = "grey80" )

title: Mean Intensity Heatmap with Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_meanmap
text: x numeric matrix data frame whose columns variables (e.g. metabolites) whose rows observations/samples. Must column names. metadata data frame number rows x valid column names. Must contain column specified group. group single character string naming column metadata defines sample groups. Group means computed per level variable used heatmap columns. group_order character vector specifying display order group levels (left right). Must contain exactly levels metadata[[group]]. NULL (default), levels ordered appear factor(metadata[[group]]). sig_vars character vector variable (column) names mark significance marker right side heatmap. Default NULL (markers). sig_marker single character string used significance marker symbol. Default "*". sig_color single character string (colour name hex) significance marker. Default "red". clust_method single character string passed method argument hclust. Default "ward.D2". dist_method single character string passed method argument dist. Default "euclidean". scale_vars single character string controlling row-wise scaling applied clustering display. One "none" (default; use values supplied), "zscore" (subtract row mean, divide row SD), "minmax" (scale row (0, 1) inclusive). n_clusters positive integer NULL. NULL, dendrogram cut n_clusters clusters using cutree, cluster coloured distinct colour-blind-friendly palette, cluster bracket + label annotations added left dendrogram. Default NULL. cluster_labels character vector labels cluster, order top plot bottom (.e. dendrogram leaf order). Must length equal n_clusters supplied. Default NULL, auto-generates labels "Cluster ", "Cluster II", etc. cluster_colors character vector colours cluster. Must length equal n_clusters supplied. Default NULL, uses Okabe-Ito colour-blind-safe palette. heatmap_colors character vector length \(\ge 2\) specifying colour gradient heatmap fill, low high values. Default c("#008000", "#FFFF00", "#FF0000") (green–yellow–red). show_values Logical. Whether print rounded mean value inside heatmap cell. Default FALSE. value_digits single non-negative integer. Number decimal places -cell value labels. Default 2. Ignored show_values = FALSE. value_color single character string -cell text colour. Default "black". global_font_size Numeric. Base font size points. Default 11. row_font_size Numeric NULL. Font size row (variable) labels right y-axis. Derived global_font_size NULL (default). col_font_size Numeric NULL. Font size column (group) labels x-axis. Derived global_font_size NULL (default). legend_font_size Numeric NULL. Font size legend text. Derived global_font_size NULL (default). cluster_label_size Numeric NULL. Font size cluster bracket labels. Derived global_font_size NULL (default). Ignored n_clusters NULL. sig_marker_size Numeric NULL. Font size significance markers. Derived global_font_size NULL (default). dend_width Numeric (0, 1). Relative width dendrogram panel fraction total plot width. Default 0.2. legend_position single character string legend placement. One "right" (default), "bottom", "left", "top", "none". na_color single character string fill colour NA cells. Default "grey80".

title: Mean Intensity Heatmap with Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_meanmap
text: patchwork object. following attributes attached: mean_matrix \(p \times g\) matrix group means (unscaled), rows dendrogram leaf order. scaled_matrix scaled version mean_matrix used clustering display (identical mean_matrix scale_vars = "none"). hclust_obj hclust object. leaf_order Integer vector row indices dendrogram leaf order. cluster_membership Named integer vector cluster assignments per variable (set n_clusters NULL).

title: Mean Intensity Heatmap with Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_meanmap
text: Algorithm overview Group mean computation: level metadata[[group]], column means x computed (ignoring NAs), producing \(p \times g\) matrix \(p\) number variables \(g\) number groups. Optional scaling: scale_vars "zscore" "minmax", row (variable) mean matrix scaled independently clustering display. Clustering: Euclidean (user-specified) distances computed rows (possibly scaled) mean matrix hierarchical clustering applied. Reordering: Rows permuted leaf order dendrogram. Dendrogram: Built via ggdendro optional cluster colouring. bracket--label annotation layer added n_clusters supplied. Heatmap: Built ggplot2::geom_tile using user-controlled colour gradient. Significance markers -cell values added optional annotation layers. Assembly: patchwork::wrap_plots() aligns two panels zero inter-panel spacing. Scaling recommendation: variables comparable scales (e.g. z-scored log-transformed), scale_vars = "none" appropriate. raw intensities different scales, use scale_vars = "zscore" make colour gradient interpretable across rows.

title: Mean Intensity Heatmap with Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_meanmap
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Mean Intensity Heatmap with Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram — plot_meanmap
code: # \donttest{ ## ── Basic example: iris mean intensity by species ──────────────────────── data(iris) num_part <- iris[, 1:4] meta_part <- data.frame(Species = iris$Species)  p <- plot_meanmap(   x         = num_part,   metadata  = meta_part,   group     = "Species",   scale_vars = "zscore" ) print(p)   ## ── With cluster colouring and significance markers ────────────────────── p2 <- plot_meanmap(   x                = num_part,   metadata         = meta_part,   group            = "Species",   scale_vars       = "zscore",   n_clusters       = 2,   cluster_labels   = c("Cluster I", "Cluster II"),   sig_vars         = c("Petal.Length", "Petal.Width"),   sig_color        = "red",   global_font_size = 11 ) print(p2)   ## ── Custom group order and colour palette ──────────────────────────────── p3 <- plot_meanmap(   x           = num_part,   metadata    = meta_part,   group       = "Species",   group_order = c("virginica", "versicolor", "setosa"),   scale_vars  = "zscore",   n_clusters  = 2,   heatmap_colors = c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B") ) print(p3)  # }

what: Plot Results from a Linear Mixed-Effects Model Analysis — plot_mixedmodel
title: Plot Results from a Linear Mixed-Effects Model Analysis — plot_mixedmodel
text: Produces volcano plot combined_fixed_effects component run_mixedmodel object, log2 fold-change (raw estimates) x-axis \(-\log_{10}(p_{\text{adj}})\) y-axis. model formula contained one fixed effect term, panels automatically faceted term predictor shown separately. Points colored significance direction, labels can added top-\(n\) features per panel user-specified set features.

title: Plot Results from a Linear Mixed-Effects Model Analysis — plot_mixedmodel
code: plot_mixedmodel(   x,   type = "volcano",   term = NULL,   use_padj = TRUE,   p_threshold = 0.05,   estimate_threshold = 0,   label_features = NULL,   minmax = NULL,   iqr_k = 1.5,   label_size = 3,   label_max_overlap = 20L,   label_box = FALSE,   label_color = "match",   point_size = 2,   point_size_sig = 2.5,   alpha = 0.6,   label_sig_only = TRUE,   color_up = "#D85A30",   color_down = "#0072B2",   color_ns = "#888780",   x_label = "Estimate (fixed effect)",   y_label = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   facet_scales = "fixed",   facet_ncol = NULL,   theme = "nature",   base_size = 12,   font_family = "",   zoom = 1 )

title: Plot Results from a Linear Mixed-Effects Model Analysis — plot_mixedmodel
text: x run_mixedmodel object returned run_mixedmodel. type Character string. Currently "volcano" supported. Reserved future types ("effect", "heatmap"). Default: "volcano". term character vector fixed-effect term name(s) include. NULL (default), non-intercept terms included. Useful focusing single predictor formula many. use_padj Logical. TRUE (default), y-axis reflects p_adj (Benjamini-Hochberg whichever method passed run_mixedmodel). FALSE, raw p_value used. p_threshold Numeric. Horizontal dashed significance line drawn \(-\log_{10}(\text{p\_threshold})\). Default: 0.05. estimate_threshold Numeric. Vertical dashed lines drawn \(\pm\)estimate_threshold. Set 0 suppress. Default: 0 (vertical lines). label_features character vector specific feature names label, regardless significance rank. Combined label_top (union). Default: NULL. minmax numeric vector length 2, e.g. c(-0.02, 0.02). Features estimate \(\le\) minmax[1] \(\ge\) minmax[2] annotated. Overrides automatic IQR-based annotation. Default: NULL. iqr_k Numeric. IQR fence multiplier used automatic annotation minmax label_features NULL. Features beyond \(Q1 - k \times IQR\) \(Q3 + k \times IQR\) labelled. Set Inf disable automatic annotation entirely. Default: 1.5. label_size Numeric. Font size point labels (ggplot2 pt units). Default: 3. label_max_overlap Integer. Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel's max.overlaps. Default: 20. label_box Logical. TRUE, draws border box around labels via ggrepel::geom_label_repel. Default: FALSE. label_color Character. Color label text. "match" inherits point's significance color. valid color string overrides. Default: "match". point_size Numeric. Base point size non-significant features. Default: 2. point_size_sig Numeric. Point size significant features. Default: 2.5. alpha Numeric (0, 1]. Transparency non-significant points. Default: 0.6. label_sig_only Logical. TRUE (default), features passing p_threshold eligible minmax IQR-based auto-annotation. Features label_features always annotated regardless setting. Default: TRUE. color_up Character. Color significantly -regulated (positive estimate + significant) features. Default: "#D85A30" (Okabe-Ito vermillion). color_down Character. Color significantly -regulated (negative estimate + significant) features. Default: "#0072B2" (Okabe-Ito blue). color_ns Character. Color non-significant features. Default: "#888780". x_label Character. x-axis label. Default: "Estimate (fixed effect)". y_label Character. y-axis label. Default: expression(-log[10](p[adj])) expression(-log[10](p)) depending use_padj. title Character. Plot title. Default: NULL (title). subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default: NULL. caption Character. Plot caption. NULL produces automatic caption reporting p-value threshold adjustment method. "" suppresses caption entirely. Default: NULL. facet_scales Character. Passed ggplot2::facet_wrap's scales argument multiple terms plotted. One "fixed", "free_x", "free_y", "free". Default: "fixed". facet_ncol Integer NULL. Number columns facet grid. NULL lets ggplot2 decide. Default: NULL. theme Character string. One "nature" (default, built theme_bw), "minimal", "classic", "bw". base_size Numeric. Base font size passed theme. Default: 12. font_family Character. Font family. Default: "" (system default). zoom Numeric scalar. Multiplies point sizes, label sizes, line widths, base font size proportionally export scaling. Default: 1.

title: Plot Results from a Linear Mixed-Effects Model Analysis — plot_mixedmodel
text: ggplot object (single-term) patchwork-composed plot (multi-term facet_wrap). object can passed save_plots() modified standard ggplot2 additions.

title: Plot Results from a Linear Mixed-Effects Model Analysis — plot_mixedmodel
text: Significance classes: feature assigned one three classes per term panel: — estimate > estimate_threshold p (adjusted raw) < p_threshold. — estimate < \(-\)estimate_threshold p < p_threshold. NS — meet either criterion. estimate_threshold = 0 (default), /split purely sign estimate — useful single continuous predictor (e.g., age, time numeric) conventional fold-change cutoff. ggrepel: ggrepel installed (strongly recommended dense plots), used automatically. Install install.packages("ggrepel"). absent, geom_text used slight upward nudge.

title: Plot Results from a Linear Mixed-Effects Model Analysis — plot_mixedmodel
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Plot Results from a Linear Mixed-Effects Model Analysis — plot_mixedmodel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- run_mixedmodel(   x        = x,   metadata = metadata,   formula  = ".feature ~ time + age + (1 | subject_id)" )  # Default: all terms, auto-label top 10 significant features plot_mixedmodel(result)  # Focus on one term, label specific features plot_mixedmodel(   result,   term           = "timepost",   label_features = c("glucose", "insulin"),   color_up       = "#CC79A7",   alpha          = 0.3,   alpha_sig      = 0.9,   title          = "Time effect (pre → post)" )  # No auto-labels, only highlight manually specified features plot_mixedmodel(   result,   label_features = c("glucose", "insulin", "PA O-28:1"),   label_box      = TRUE,   label_color    = "black" ) } # }

what: Plot Multiple Variables on Primary and Secondary Y Axes — plot_multi_y
title: Plot Multiple Variables on Primary and Secondary Y Axes — plot_multi_y
text: Creates dual-axis ggplot shared x-axis, allowing simultaneous display multiple variables independent primary secondary y scales. Supports four geometry types (line, area, histogram, dot) axis, optional faceting, colorblind-safe palettes, full publication-ready typography controls.

title: Plot Multiple Variables on Primary and Secondary Y Axes — plot_multi_y
code: plot_multi_y(   x,   x_axis,   main_as_line = NULL,   main_as_area = NULL,   main_as_hist = NULL,   main_as_dot = NULL,   secondary_as_line = NULL,   secondary_as_area = NULL,   secondary_as_hist = NULL,   secondary_as_dot = NULL,   order_main = c("hist", "area", "line", "dot"),   order_secondary = c("hist", "area", "line", "dot"),   color_main_line = NULL,   color_main_area = NULL,   color_main_hist = NULL,   color_main_dot = NULL,   color_secondary_line = NULL,   color_secondary_area = NULL,   color_secondary_hist = NULL,   color_secondary_dot = NULL,   linewidth_main_line = 0.8,   linewidth_secondary_line = 0.8,   size_main_dot = 2,   size_secondary_dot = 2,   area_alpha_main = 0.35,   area_alpha_secondary = 0.35,   facet = NULL,   facet_ncol = NULL,   legend_pos = "top",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   x_lab = NULL,   y_lab_main = NULL,   y_lab_secondary = "Secondary axis",   global_font_size = 20,   title_font_size = NULL,   subtitle_font_size = NULL,   caption_font_size = NULL,   axis_font_size = NULL,   legend_font_size = NULL,   facet_font_size = NULL,   theme = "nature",   zoom = 1 )

title: Plot Multiple Variables on Primary and Secondary Y Axes — plot_multi_y
text: x data.frame, tibble, matrix (converted internally). Column names may contain special characters. x_axis single character string giving column x use x-axis. May numeric, Date/POSIXct, categorical. main_as_line Character vector column name(s) plot line geometry primary y-axis. NULL omits geometry. main_as_area Character vector column name(s) plot area geometry primary y-axis. NULL omits geometry. main_as_hist Character vector column name(s) plot histogram (bar) geometry primary y-axis. NULL omits geometry. main_as_dot Character vector column name(s) plot point geometry primary y-axis. NULL omits geometry. secondary_as_line Character vector column name(s) plot line geometry secondary y-axis. NULL omits geometry. secondary_as_area Character vector column name(s) plot area geometry secondary y-axis. NULL omits geometry. secondary_as_hist Character vector column name(s) plot histogram (bar) geometry secondary y-axis. NULL omits geometry. secondary_as_dot Character vector column name(s) plot point geometry secondary y-axis. NULL omits geometry. order_main Character vector specifying back--front layer order primary geometries. Default c("hist", "area", "line", "dot"). order_secondary order_main secondary geometries. color_main_line Character vector R color names hex codes primary line geometry. Recycled fewer colors series. Defaults Okabe-Ito colorblind-safe palette. color_main_area Character vector colors primary area geometry. color_main_hist Character vector colors primary histogram geometry. color_main_dot Character vector colors primary dot geometry. color_secondary_line Character vector colors secondary line geometry. color_secondary_area Character vector colors secondary area geometry. color_secondary_hist Character vector colors secondary histogram geometry. color_secondary_dot Character vector colors secondary dot geometry. linewidth_main_line Numeric. Line width primary line geometry. Default 0.8. linewidth_secondary_line Numeric. Line width secondary line geometry. Default 0.8. size_main_dot Numeric. Point size primary dot geometry. Default 2. size_secondary_dot Numeric. Point size secondary dot geometry. Default 2. area_alpha_main Numeric \([0, 1]\). Fill transparency primary area histogram geometries. Default 0.35. area_alpha_secondary Numeric \([0, 1]\). Fill transparency secondary area histogram geometries. Default 0.35. facet single character string naming categorical column x use facet_wrap. NULL (default) disables faceting. facet_ncol Integer. Number columns facet set. NULL lets ggplot2 choose automatically. legend_pos Character. Legend position: "top" (default), "bottom", "left", "right". title Character. Plot title. NULL omits title. subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. NULL omits subtitle. caption Character. Plot caption. NULL omits caption. x_lab Character. x-axis label. Defaults value x_axis. y_lab_main Character. Primary y-axis label. Defaults "Primary axis" secondary variables supplied, otherwise empty string. y_lab_secondary Character. Secondary y-axis label. Defaults "Secondary axis". global_font_size Numeric. Base font size points. Default 20. title_font_size Numeric. Override font size plot title. Defaults 1.20 * global_font_size. subtitle_font_size Numeric. Override font size subtitle. title: Plot Multiple Variables on Primary and Secondary Y Axes — plot_multi_y
text: Dual-axis implementation. ggplot2 implements secondary axes linear rescalings primary axis (sec_axis). plot_multi_y computes linear transform mapping secondary variables' observed range primary range 5\ NA handling. Rows x_axis NA always dropped plotting. Rows y-variable NA retained series x position still drawn; ggplot2 renders gaps series naturally.  warning issued listing columns contain NA values remain aware gaps. Long-format pivoting. geometry group pivoted long format internally, .series factor carrying original column name. feeds colour/fill aesthetics drives legend, ensuring manual colour scale always matching levels. Layer ordering. order_main order_secondary control painter's order. default c("hist", "area", "line", "dot") places histograms back, areas, lines, dots foreground.

title: Plot Multiple Variables on Primary and Secondary Y Axes — plot_multi_y

title: Plot Multiple Variables on Primary and Secondary Y Axes — plot_multi_y
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # --- Basic: area on primary, two lines on primary, faceted by Year --- set.seed(42) df <- data.frame(   Year         = factor(rep(c("2021", "2022"), each = 20)),   `Week No.`   = rep(1:20, 2),   `Weekly cases` = c(rpois(20, 60), rpois(20, 80)),   `RdRP GC/L`  = c(rep(NA, 5), runif(15, 1e4, 1e5),                    rep(NA, 3), runif(17, 2e4, 2e5)),   `N GC/L`     = c(rep(NA, 8), runif(12, 5e3, 8e4),                    rep(NA, 4), runif(16, 1e4, 1e5)),   check.names  = FALSE )  plot_multi_y(   x             = df,   x_axis        = "Week No.",   main_as_area  = "Weekly cases",   main_as_line  = c("RdRP GC/L", "N GC/L"),   facet         = "Year",   title         = NULL )  # --- Dual axis: cases on primary, viral load on secondary --- plot_multi_y(   x                  = df,   x_axis             = "Week No.",   main_as_area       = "Weekly cases",   secondary_as_line  = c("RdRP GC/L", "N GC/L"),   y_lab_main         = "Weekly cases",   y_lab_secondary    = "GC/L",   facet              = "Year" ) } # }

what: Plot Relative Abundance as Stacked Bar Chart — plot_relabund
title: Plot Relative Abundance as Stacked Bar Chart — plot_relabund
text: Generates stacked bar chart relative abundances feature matrix, optionally restricting display top bottom limit features total abundance collapsing remainder "Others" category. Samples may shown individually aggregated grouping variable using mean median. horizontal vertical orientations supported, along full control colours, fonts, legend layout, axis labels. Column names containing spaces special characters fully supported.

title: Plot Relative Abundance as Stacked Bar Chart — plot_relabund
code: plot_relabund(   x,   metadata,   limit = 10L,   sort = NULL,   others_position = NULL,   show_x_as = NULL,   group_aggregate = NULL,   normalize_to_relabund = TRUE,   ignore_normalization = FALSE,   order = NULL,   angle = NULL,   abundance_in_y = TRUE,   legend_position = NULL,   legend_nrow = NULL,   legend_ncol = NULL,   italicize_legend_items = FALSE,   exclude_others = FALSE,   unknown_before_others = TRUE,   theme = "nature",   colors = NULL,   plot_title = "",   plot_subtitle = "",   legend_title = "",   xlab = NULL,   ylab = "Relative abundance",   global_font_size = 20,   font_family = "sans",   title_size = NULL,   subtitle_size = NULL,   legend_size = NULL,   xlab_size = NULL,   ylab_size = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot Relative Abundance as Stacked Bar Chart — plot_relabund
text: x data.frame, tibble, matrix named columns representing features (e.g., taxa, metabolites). Rows samples; columns features. Column names may contain spaces special characters. group_aggregate = NULL ignore_normalization = FALSE, x expected already contain row-wise relative abundances (row summing 1). warning issued column whose sum 0 1, indicating column-wise relative abundance normalisation may applied. metadata data.frame containing sample-level metadata. Must number rows x; row order assumed correspond x. limit Integer NULL. Number features display, selected total abundance across samples. sort = "high" (NULL/"alphanumeric" limit), top limit highest-total features shown rest collapsed "Others". sort = "low", bottom limit lowest-total features shown instead. NULL, features plotted. Default 10. sort Character NULL. Controls features selected (limit set) order features appear legend/stack. NULL (default) preserves original column order x. Options: "alphanumeric" (lexicographic), "high" (highest total abundance first — selects top limit), "low" (lowest total abundance first — selects bottom limit). Note: "high" "low" rank column totals meaningful x contains raw counts intensities (row sums already 0 1). normalised data detected, warning issued sort ignored. using ranked sorting, supply raw count data set normalize_to_relabund = TRUE. others_position Character NULL. place "Others" category legend/stack order. NULL (default) treats "Others" according sort. "first" forces top; "last" forces bottom. show_x_as Character NULL. Column name metadata use x-axis variable (sample IDs group labels). NULL, function searches "UID", "ID", "SampleID" (case-insensitive) metadata. error raised none found. group_aggregate Character NULL. "mean" "median", show_x_as treated grouping variable raw feature values x aggregated per group normalising relative abundances. Assumes x contains raw (unnormalised) data. See normalize_to_relabund. NULL (default), row x individual sample x must already contain row-wise relative abundances (unless ignore_normalization = TRUE). normalize_to_relabund Logical. Relevant group_aggregate non-NULL sort "high"/"low". TRUE (default), run_normalize(method = "col_rel_abundance") applied x aggregation ranking. Set FALSE x already appropriately normalised. Ignored ignore_normalization = TRUE. ignore_normalization Logical. TRUE, normalisation checks pre-normalisation steps bypassed x plotted supplied. Default FALSE. Note: Setting TRUE means plotted values relative abundances; use plotting already-transformed data CLR-transformed values data originated normalised data visualised -. order Character vector, "alphanumeric", NULL. Left--right order samples/groups x-axis. character vector gives explicit sequence. "alphanumeric" sorts lexicographically. NULL (default) preserves natural order appearance metadata. angle Numeric NULL. Rotation angle (0–360 degrees) sample/group tick labels. NULL (default): 0 group_aggregate non-NULL, 45 otherwise. abundance_in_y Logical. TRUE (default), relative abundance y-axis samples/groups x-axis. FALSE, axes flipped: relative abundance x-axis, samples/groups y-axis. legend order also reversed FALSE top--stack feature appears first (top) legend. legend_position Character NULL. One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". Auto-detected NULL: "top" abundance_in_y = TRUE; "right" abundance_in_y = FALSE. legend_nrow Integer NULL. Rows legend key grid. legend_ncol Integer NULL. Columns legend key grid. italicize_legend_items Logical. Render legend labels italic. Requires ggtext; falls back element_text(face = "italic") warning. Default FALSE. exclude_others Logical. TRUE, "Others" category omitted plot entirely. Default FALSE. unknown_before_others Logical. TRUE (default), feature whose name exactly "unknown" (case-insensitive, e.g., "Unknown", "UNKNOWN", "unknown") placed immediately "Others" legend/stack, last exclude_others = TRUE. Partial matches considered; column names entirely word "unknown" affected. theme Character. ggplot2 theme. One "nature", "minimal", "classic", "bw", "light", "dark". Default "nature". colors Character vector NULL. Custom fill colours. Length must equal number plotted fill levels. Named vectors matched name. NULL uses Okabe-Ito palette (extended HCL colours). plot_title Character. Main title. Supports ggtext markdown: **bold**, *italic*. Default "". plot_subtitle Character. Subtitle. Supports markdown. Default "". legend_title Character. Legend title. Default "". xlab Character NULL. X-axis label. Defaults show_x_as. ylab Character. Y-axis label. Default "Relative abundance". global_font_size Numeric NULL. Scales text elements proportionally relative internal 11 pt reference. Default 20. font_family Character. Font family text. Default "sans". title_size Numeric NULL. Title font size (pt). subtitle_size Numeric NULL. Subtitle font size (pt). legend_size Numeric NULL. Legend title font size (pt). xlab_size Numeric NULL. X-axis label font size (pt). ylab_size Numeric NULL. Y-axis label font size (pt). ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::geom_bar().
