title: Plot Relative Abundance as Stacked Bar Chart — plot_relabund
text: Plot Relative Abundance Stacked Bar Chart Special characters column names. Column names containing spaces, slashes, parentheses, special characters fully supported. function avoids stats::reshape() (can mangle names) instead builds long-format data frame manually. Feature selection limit. Features ranked column total across samples (aggregated groups). sort = "high" NULL "alphanumeric", top limit features total retained; others collapsed "Others". sort = "low", bottom limit features total retained instead. Normalisation flow. ignore_normalization = FALSE normalize_to_relabund = TRUE, row-relative abundance applied via run_normalize(method = "row_rel_abundance") aggregation ranking. Row-wise proportions computed bar sums 100\ ignore_normalization = TRUE, data plotted supplied y-axis label interpreted accordingly. Legend reversal flipped. abundance_in_y = FALSE, coord_flip() causes stack render left--right. legend fill levels reversed first (top--stack) feature also appears first (top) legend, maintaining visual correspondence.

title: Plot Relative Abundance as Stacked Bar Chart — plot_relabund
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Plot Relative Abundance as Stacked Bar Chart — plot_relabund
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(pondeR) set.seed(42) n    <- 20 taxa <- c("Firmicutes", "Bacteroidetes", "Proteobacteria (class A)",           "Actino/bacteroides", "Verrucomicrobia", "Fusobacteria sp.",           "Tenericutes", "Spirochaetes", "Unknown", "Cyanobacteria",           "Chloroflexi", "Acidobacteria")  counts <- as.data.frame(   matrix(sample(0L:5000L, n * length(taxa), replace = TRUE),          nrow = n, dimnames = list(NULL, taxa)) ) meta <- data.frame(   SampleID = paste0("S", seq_len(n)),   Group    = rep(c("Control", "Treatment"), each = n / 2L) )  # --- Pipeline: col_rel_abundance → CLR → plot as-is --- norm_out <- run_normalize(counts, meta, method = "col_rel_abundance",                           verbose = FALSE) clr_out  <- run_transform(norm_out$data, method = "clr")  plot_relabund(   x                    = clr_out$data,   metadata             = meta,   limit                = 10L,   ignore_normalization = TRUE,   plot_title           = "Gut Microbiome (CLR-transformed)" )  # --- Group-aggregated from raw counts, bold title --- plot_relabund(   x               = counts,   metadata        = meta,   show_x_as       = "Group",   group_aggregate = "mean",   order           = c("Treatment", "Control"),   limit           = 8L,   sort            = "high",   plot_title      = "**Relative Abundance** by Group",   legend_title    = "Phylum" )  # --- Horizontal bars --- plot_relabund(   x                     = counts,   metadata              = meta,   show_x_as             = "Group",   group_aggregate       = "mean",   limit                 = 6L,   exclude_others        = FALSE,   unknown_before_others = TRUE,   abundance_in_y        = FALSE ) } # }

what: Scores Plot for Multivariate Ordination Results — plot_score
title: Scores Plot for Multivariate Ordination Results — plot_score
text: Produces publication-ready scores plot output multivariate ordination dimensionality-reduction methods, including Principal Component Analysis (run_pca), Partial Least Squares PLS-DA (run_pls), Principal Coordinate Analysis (run_pcoa). Supports discrete group coloring confidence ellipses, continuous gradient coloring, outlier detection labeling, range publication themes.

title: Scores Plot for Multivariate Ordination Results — plot_score
code: plot_score(res, ...)  # S3 method for class 'run_pca' plot_score(   res,   pc = c(1, 2),   color_by,   points_from,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   position = "center",   arrange_levels = NULL,   ellipse = TRUE,   ellipse_type = "t",   ellipse_level = 0.95,   legend = NULL,   legend_position = "bottom",   show_outliers = FALSE,   label_outliers = "all",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 10,   point_alpha = 0.8,   theme = "nature",   base_size = 30,   font_family = "sans",   axis_title_size = NULL,   axis_text_size = NULL,   plot_title_size = NULL,   legend_title_size = NULL,   legend_text_size = NULL,   zoom = 1,   verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'run_pls' plot_score(   res,   pc = c(1, 2),   color_by,   points_from,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   position = "center",   arrange_levels = NULL,   ellipse = TRUE,   ellipse_type = "t",   ellipse_level = 0.95,   legend = NULL,   legend_position = "bottom",   show_outliers = FALSE,   label_outliers = "all",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 10,   point_alpha = 0.8,   theme = "nature",   base_size = 30,   font_family = "sans",   axis_title_size = NULL,   axis_text_size = NULL,   plot_title_size = NULL,   legend_title_size = NULL,   legend_text_size = NULL,   zoom = 1,   verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'run_pcoa' plot_score(   res,   metadata,   pc = c(1, 2),   color_by,   points_from,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   position = "center",   arrange_levels = NULL,   ellipse = TRUE,   ellipse_type = "t",   ellipse_level = 0.95,   legend = NULL,   legend_position = "bottom",   show_outliers = FALSE,   label_outliers = "all",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3,   point_alpha = 0.8,   theme = "nature",   base_size = 11,   font_family = "sans",   axis_title_size = NULL,   axis_text_size = NULL,   plot_title_size = NULL,   legend_title_size = NULL,   legend_text_size = NULL,   zoom = 1,   verbose = TRUE )  # Default S3 method plot_score(res, ...)

title: Scores Plot for Multivariate Ordination Results — plot_score
text: res List. Output run_pca(), run_pls(), run_pcoa(). appropriate plot method selected automatically via S3 dispatch based class res. pc Character vector length 2. Principal components plot, e.g., c("PC1", "PC2"). Can also numeric, e.g., c(1, 2). Default: c(1, 2). color_by Character. Name column metadata use coloring points. column contains categorical data, points colored discrete groups optional confidence ellipses. column contains numeric data, points colored using continuous gradient scale. Required parameter default. points_from Character. Name column metadata use labeling points. determines values used sample labels plot outlier labeling enabled. specified column found metadata, sequential labels used instead. Required parameter default. title Character. Main plot title. Default: auto-generated based PCs. subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default: NULL (subtitle). caption Character. Plot caption (bottom-right). Default: NULL (caption). position Character. Horizontal alignment title, subtitle, caption. Options: "left", "center", "right". Default: "center". arrange_levels Character vector. Custom order group levels legend (applies categorical color_by). Default: NULL (alphabetical order). ellipse Logical. Draw confidence ellipses around groups. applies color_by categorical. Requires least 3 samples per group. Default: TRUE. ellipse_type Character. Type ellipse: "t" (t-distribution, default) "norm" (normal distribution). Default: "t". ellipse_level Numeric. Confidence level ellipses (0–1). Default: 0.95. legend Character NULL. Custom legend title. Default: NULL (uses color_by column name). legend_position Character. Position legend. Options: "bottom", "top", "left", "right", "none". Default: "bottom". show_outliers Logical. Label samples outside confidence ellipse defined ellipse_level. works ellipses drawn (categorical coloring). Default: FALSE. label_outliers Character. groups check outliers. Use "" (default) character vector specific group names color_by variable. colors Character vector. Custom color palette discrete groups. NULL (default), Okabe-Ito colorblind-friendly palette used. Ignored color_by numeric (continuous scale always used numeric variables). point_size Numeric. Size points. Default: 10. point_alpha Numeric. Transparency points (0–1). Default: 0.8. theme Character. ggplot2 theme apply. Options: "nature", "minimal", "classic", "bw", "light", "dark". Default: "nature" (clean, publication-ready style based theme_bw()). base_size Numeric. Base font size theme (pts). Default: 30. font_family Character. Font family text elements. Default: "sans". axis_title_size Numeric. Font size axis titles. Default: base_size + 1. axis_text_size Numeric. Font size axis tick labels. Default: base_size - 1. plot_title_size Numeric. Font size plot title. Default: base_size + 3. legend_title_size Numeric. Font size legend title. Default: base_size. legend_text_size Numeric. Font size legend text. Default: base_size - 1. zoom Numeric. Scaling factor applied uniformly text point size elements. Values > 1 enlarge, values < 1 shrink. Default: 1. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE. metadata data.frame one row per observation (sample). Required plot_score.run_pcoa run_pcoa() bundle metadata inside result (unlike run_pca()). Row order must match row order data matrix originally passed run_pcoa().

title: Scores Plot for Multivariate Ordination Results — plot_score
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Scores Plot for Multivariate Ordination Results — plot_score
text: Scores Plot Interpretation: Clustering: Samples cluster together similar metabolic profiles. Separation: Distance groups indicates metabolic differences. Outliers: Samples far group may represent biological outliers, technical issues, mislabeled samples. Coloring Options: color_by parameter determines points colored: Categorical column: Colors discrete groups (e.g., Treatment, Genotype). Points colored shaped variable. Confidence ellipses can drawn ellipse = TRUE. Outliers can detected labeled. Colors default Okabe-Ito colorblind-safe palette; override colors. Numeric column: Gradient coloring continuous variable (e.g., Time, Dose, Age). Uses viridis "plasma" color scale. Points shaped variable (binned quartiles). ellipses outlier detection available. Color Defaults: default, discrete groups use Okabe-Ito palette, perceptually distinct safe common forms color vision deficiency (deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia). palette supports 8 groups. 8 groups, supply custom colors vector. Point Labeling: points_from parameter controls samples labeled: specified column exists metadata, values used labels. column exist, sequential labels (Sample_1, Sample_2, ...) used. Labels shown show_outliers = TRUE outlier samples . Common choices: "Sample", "SampleID", "SubjectID". Outlier Detection: Outliers identified using Mahalanobis distance group centroid. sample flagged outlier falls outside confidence region defined ellipse_level. default (0.95) identifies samples outside 95% confidence region. exactly mirrors ggplot2::stat_ellipse() behaviour. Outlier detection requires: Categorical color_by ellipse = TRUE least 3 samples per group Sample Exclusion: exclude samples (e.g., QC samples) plot, exclude PCA computation using run_pca(exclude = ...) rather filtering plot stage. Themes: "nature" theme (default) clean, publication-ready style white background, minimal gridlines, top/right panel border — suitable journal figures. options map directly ggplot2 equivalents.

title: Scores Plot for Multivariate Ordination Results — plot_score
text: Mardia, K.V., Kent, J.T., & Bibby, J.M. (1979). Multivariate Analysis. London: Academic Press. Brereton, R.G., & Lloyd, G.R. (2014). Partial least squares discriminant analysis: taking magic away. Journal Chemometrics, 28(4), 213–225. doi:10.1002/cem.2609 Okabe, M., & Ito, K. (2002). Color Universal Design (CUD): make figures presentations friendly colorblind people. https://jfly.uni-koeln.de/color/

title: Scores Plot for Multivariate Ordination Results — plot_score
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Scores Plot for Multivariate Ordination Results — plot_score
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simulate data set.seed(123) x <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100, sd = 20),             nrow = 100, ncol = 50) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50) rownames(x) <- paste0("Sample", 1:100)  metadata <- data.frame(   Sample = paste0("Sample", 1:100),   Group  = rep(c("Control", "Treatment", "QC"), c(40, 40, 20)),   Batch  = rep(1:4, each = 25),   Time   = rep(c(0, 6, 12, 24), 25) )  # Run PCA (excluding QC samples) scaled_result <- run_scale(x, method = "auto") res    <- run_pca(scaled_result$data, metadata,                          group   = "Group",                          exclude = "QC")  # Basic scores plot — nature theme, Okabe-Ito colors (defaults) plot_score(res,            color_by    = "Group",            points_from = "Sample")  # PC2 vs PC3 with left-aligned title plot_score(res,            pc          = c(2, 3),            color_by    = "Group",            points_from = "Sample",            title       = "PCA: PC2 vs PC3",            position    = "left")  # Color by batch with custom palette plot_score(res,            color_by    = "Batch",            points_from = "Sample",            colors      = c("#E41A1C", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A", "#984EA3"))  # Continuous coloring by time plot_score(res,            color_by    = "Time",            points_from = "Sample",            legend      = "Collection Time (h)")  # Show outliers in specific groups, classic theme, larger zoom plot_score(res,            color_by       = "Group",            points_from    = "Sample",            show_outliers  = TRUE,            label_outliers = c("Control", "Treatment"),            ellipse_level  = 0.95,            theme          = "classic",            zoom           = 1.2)  # Stricter outlier detection with serif font plot_score(res,            color_by      = "Group",            points_from   = "Sample",            show_outliers = TRUE,            ellipse_level = 0.99,            font_family   = "serif",            base_size     = 13) } # }

title: Plot PCA Scree Plot — plot_scree

title: Plot PCA Scree Plot — plot_scree
code: plot_scree(   pca_result,   title = "Scree Plot",   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   position = "center",   what = "variance",   type = "line",   max_pc = 15,   show_cumulative = TRUE,   show_values = TRUE,   bar_color = "#0072B2",   line_color = "#0072B2",   cumulative_color = "#D55E00",   theme = "nature",   base_size = 11,   font_family = "sans",   axis_title_size = NULL,   axis_text_size = NULL,   plot_title_size = NULL,   zoom = 1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Plot PCA Scree Plot — plot_scree
text: pca_result List. Output run_pca(). title Character. Main plot title. Default: "Scree Plot". subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default: NULL (subtitle). caption Character. Plot caption (bottom-right). Default: NULL (caption). position Character. Horizontal alignment title, subtitle, caption. Options: "left", "center", "right". Default: "center". Character. plot: "variance" (variance explained %) "eigen" (eigenvalues). Default: "variance". type Character. Plot geometry: "bar", "line", "". Default: "line". max_pc Integer. Maximum number PCs display. Default: 15. show_cumulative Logical. Show cumulative variance explained dashed line secondary y-axis. available = "variance". Default: TRUE. show_values Logical. Display value labels plot using ggrepel avoid overlap. Falls back geom_text ggrepel installed. Default: TRUE. bar_color Character. Fill color bars (type = "bar" ""). Default: "#0072B2" (Okabe-Ito blue). line_color Character. Color primary line points (type = "line" ""). Default: "#0072B2" (Okabe-Ito blue). cumulative_color Character. Color cumulative variance line, points, labels. Default: "#D55E00" (Okabe-Ito vermillion). theme Character. ggplot2 theme apply. Options: "nature", "minimal", "classic", "bw", "light", "dark". Default: "nature" (clean, publication-ready style based theme_bw() gridlines). base_size Numeric. Base font size theme (pts). Default: 11. font_family Character. Font family text elements. Default: "sans". axis_title_size Numeric. Font size axis titles. Default: base_size + 1. axis_text_size Numeric. Font size axis tick labels. Default: base_size - 1. plot_title_size Numeric. Font size plot title. Default: base_size + 3. zoom Numeric. Uniform scaling factor applied text sizes point/line sizes. Values > 1 enlarge, values < 1 shrink. Default: 1. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Plot PCA Scree Plot — plot_scree
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Plot PCA Scree Plot — plot_scree
text: Scree Plot Interpretation: scree plot helps determine many principal components retain: Elbow Method: Look "elbow" curve flattens. PCs elbow capture meaningful variance; capture mostly noise. Kaiser Criterion: Retain PCs eigenvalues > 1 (= "eigen"). PCs explain variance single original variable. Cumulative Variance: Retain enough PCs explain 70–90% total variance (shown show_cumulative = TRUE). Plot Types: "bar": Traditional bar chart; good discrete comparison. "line": Line plot; better visualising trends elbow. "": Combines bars line comprehensive view. Cumulative Variance Line: show_cumulative = TRUE, dashed line showing cumulative variance overlaid shared y-axis scale. secondary axis right labelled accordingly. available = "variance". Use Metric: Variance explained: intuitive (percentages). Recommended users. Eigenvalues: Useful Kaiser criterion advanced analysis. Themes: "nature" theme (default) applies theme_bw() gridlines removed — suitable journal figures. options map directly ggplot2 equivalents, also gridlines suppressed.

title: Plot PCA Scree Plot — plot_scree
text: Cattell, R.B. (1966). scree test number factors. Multivariate Behavioral Research, 1(2), 245–276. doi:10.1207/s15327906mbr0102_10 Kaiser, H.F. (1960). application electronic computers factor analysis. Educational Psychological Measurement, 20(1), 141–151. doi:10.1177/001316446002000116

title: Plot PCA Scree Plot — plot_scree
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run PCA first pca_result <- run_pca(scaled_data, metadata, scale_method = "auto")  # Basic scree plot (nature theme, Okabe-Ito colors, defaults) plot_scree(pca_result)  # Eigenvalue plot, bar + line geometry plot_scree(pca_result,            what     = "eigen",            type     = "both",            title    = "PCA Eigenvalues",            position = "left")  # Variance plot without cumulative line, classic theme plot_scree(pca_result,            show_cumulative = FALSE,            max_pc          = 10,            theme           = "classic")  # Larger font, serif family, custom zoom plot_scree(pca_result,            base_size   = 13,            font_family = "serif",            zoom        = 1.2) } # }

what: Volcano Plot for Fold Change and p-value Data — plot_volcano
title: Volcano Plot for Fold Change and p-value Data — plot_volcano
text: Generates publication-quality volcano plots visualise differential features scatter log2 fold change (x-axis) versus statistical significance (y-axis). Designed integrate directly output run_foldchange() run_diff(), accepts data.frame required columns. group supplied, comparisons overlaid single plot distinct colours per group, saving publication space.

title: Volcano Plot for Fold Change and p-value Data — plot_volcano

Column names may contain special characters. List input — supported combinations: list(fc = <run_foldchange>, diff = <run_diff>) objects supplied. Fold changes set 1 (\(\log_2 \text{FC} = 0\)) features; x-axis meaningful. warning issued. list supplied, arguments y, z, features, group set automatically need specified caller (though can overridden). y single character string naming column x contains log2 fold change values (numeric). Note thresholds specified raw fold changes (e.g., = 1.5), converted log2 scale internally applied column. z single character string naming column x contains raw p-values (numeric, \([0, 1]\)). group optional single character string naming column x identifies comparison group row belongs (e.g. "Severe_vs_Healthy"). supplied, groups overlaid one plot coloured group. Default NULL. features single character string naming column x contains feature identifiers. Required annotate NULL. single numeric value greater 1. Features fold change \(\ge \text{}\) \(p < \text{pval}\) coloured -regulated. Default 1.5 (.e., 1.5-fold increase). single numeric value \((0, 1)\). Features fold change \(\le \text{}\) \(p < \text{pval}\) coloured -regulated. example, = 0.667 corresponds ~1.5-fold decrease. Default 0.5. pval single numeric value \((0, 1]\). Default 0.05. annotate optional vector feature identifiers label plot. Values must found column specified features. May plain character vector (labels shown -) named character vector created setNames(feature_ids, display_names), case display names shown plot. line segment always connects label corresponding point. Default NULL. use_ggrepel Logical. TRUE (default), uses ggrepel::geom_text_repel() non-overlapping labels connector segments. FALSE, uses ggplot2::geom_text(). up_color Character vector colours -regulated points. group NULL first element used (default "red"). group supplied, one colour per group level used; fewer colours levels provided recycled warning. Default c("red", "purple", "yellow"). down_color Character vector colours -regulated points. recycling behaviour up_color. Default c("blue", "green", "cyan"). ns_color Colour non-significant points. Default "grey60". neglog10 Logical. TRUE (default), y-axis shows \(-\log_{10}(p)\); FALSE, raw p-values plotted inverted y-axis. h_color Colour horizontal significance threshold line. title: Volcano Plot for Fold Change and p-value Data — plot_volcano

title: Volcano Plot for Fold Change and p-value Data — plot_volcano
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Volcano Plot for Fold Change and p-value Data — plot_volcano
code: ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 1 — minimal synthetic data, no group ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- set.seed(42) n   <- 200 dat <- data.frame(   feature = paste0("feat_", seq_len(n)),   log2fc  = rnorm(n, 0, 2),   pvalue  = runif(n, 0, 0.5) )  res <- plot_volcano(x = dat, y = "log2fc", z = "pvalue") #> Error: Column `y` ("log2fc") contains non-positive values. Fold changes must be positive (e.g., 0.5 for a 2-fold decrease). print(res$plots[[1]]) #> Error: object 'res' not found  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 2 — asymmetric thresholds as raw fold changes, with labels ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- ann <- setNames(c("feat_1", "feat_2", "feat_3"),                 c("Marker A", "Marker B", "Marker C"))  res2 <- plot_volcano(   x        = dat,   y        = "log2fc",   z        = "pvalue",   features = "feature",   up       = 1.5,   down     = 1/1.5,   annotate = ann ) #> Error: Column `y` ("log2fc") contains non-positive values. Fold changes must be positive (e.g., 0.5 for a 2-fold decrease). print(res2$plots[[1]]) #> Error: object 'res2' not found  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 3 — multi-group overlay ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- set.seed(7) dat_multi <- data.frame(   feature    = rep(paste0("feat_", seq_len(50)), 3),   log2fc     = rnorm(150, 0, 1.5),   pvalue     = runif(150, 0, 0.3),   comparison = rep(c("Severe_vs_Healthy", "Nonsevere_vs_Healthy",                      "Severe_vs_Nonsevere"), each = 50) )  res3 <- plot_volcano(   x           = dat_multi,   y           = "log2fc",   z           = "pvalue",   group       = "comparison",   features    = "feature",   annotate    = setNames(c("feat_1", "feat_5"), c("Marker A", "Marker B")),   alpha       = 0.5,   legend_nrow = 2 ) #> Error: Column `y` ("log2fc") contains non-positive values. Fold changes must be positive (e.g., 0.5 for a 2-fold decrease). print(res3$plots[[1]]) #> Error: object 'res3' not found

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: Runs complete, sequential preprocessing pipeline metabolomics data orchestrating individual run_* functions pondeR. Steps execute fixed, reproducible order regardless argument arrangement.

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
code: run_DIpreprocess(   x,   metadata,   sample_id_col = "Sample",   group_col = "Group",   qc_types = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   batch_col = "Batch",   injection_col = "InjectionSequence",   norm_factor_col = "Normalization",   subject_id_col = "SubjectID",   outliers = NULL,   missing_threshold = 0.2,   missing_by_group = TRUE,   missing_include_qc = FALSE,   impute_fraction = 0.2,   positive_only = TRUE,   correct_drift = TRUE,   remove_uncorrected = FALSE,   spline_smooth = 0,   spline_spar_limit = c(-1.5, 1.5),   correct_on_log = TRUE,   min_qc_per_batch = 5L,   normalize_method = "sum",   normalize_ref_sample = NULL,   normalize_qc_method = "median",   transform_method = "log2",   vsn_cores = 1L,   scale_nonpls = "auto",   scale_pls = "pareto",   rsd_threshold = 0.3,   rsd_qc_type = "EQC",   variance_percentile = 10,   scale_filter_ref = "auto",   merge_replicates = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: x Matrix data frame. Numeric feature data samples rows features columns. values non-negative raw intensities; zeros treated missing internally. metadata Data frame. Sample metadata nrow(metadata) == nrow(x). minimum, group column (default "Group") must present. sample_id_col Character. Column metadata containing unique sample identifiers match rownames(x). Default: "Sample". group_col Character. Column metadata containing group labels. Default: "Group". qc_types Character vector. Group labels identify QC samples. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). batch_col Character. Column metadata containing batch numbers. Required drift correction. Default: "Batch". injection_col Character. Column metadata containing injection order (integer). Required drift correction. Default: "InjectionSequence". norm_factor_col Character. Column metadata containing external normalization factors. Default: "Normalization". subject_id_col Character. Column metadata containing subject identifiers technical-replicate merging. Default: "SubjectID". outliers Character vector NULL. Row names x remove processing. Default: NULL. missing_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. Missing value threshold. Default: 0.2. missing_by_group Logical. TRUE, assess per group. Default: TRUE. missing_include_qc Logical. TRUE, include QC samples. Default: FALSE. impute_fraction Numeric > 0. Fraction smallest positive value per feature used imputation. Default: 0.2. positive_only Logical. TRUE, imputation considers positive values. Default: TRUE. correct_drift Logical. TRUE, apply QCRSC correction. Default: TRUE. remove_uncorrected Logical. TRUE, remove features QCRSC correct. Default: FALSE. spline_smooth Numeric [0, 1]. Smoothing parameter. Default: 0. spline_spar_limit Numeric vector. Lower/upper bounds spline. Default: c(-1.5, 1.5). correct_on_log Logical. TRUE, fit log-transformed data. Default: TRUE. min_qc_per_batch Integer. Minimum QC samples per batch. Default: 5. normalize_method Character. Normalization method. "sum": Normalizes sum. "median": Normalizes median. "specific_factor": Uses external factors norm_factor_col. "pqn_global": PQN using global reference. "pqn_reference": PQN using normalize_ref_sample. "pqn_group": PQN grouping. "quantile": Quantile normalization. "none": normalization. Default: "sum". normalize_ref_sample Character. Reference sample name PQN. Default: NULL. normalize_qc_method Character. QC normalization using factors. One "mean", "median", "none". Default: "median". transform_method Character. Transformation method. "log2", "log10", "sqrt", "cbrt", "clr", "vsn", "glog", "none". Default: "log2". vsn_cores Integer "max". Cores VSN. Default: 1. scale_nonpls Character. Scaling NONPLS branch ("auto", "pareto", "mean"). scale_pls Character. Scaling PLS branch ("pareto", "auto", "mean"). rsd_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. QC-RSD threshold. Default: 0.3. rsd_qc_type Character. QC type RSD ("EQC", "SQC", "QC"). variance_percentile Numeric [0, 100]. Percentile filter. Default: 10. scale_filter_ref Character. Harmonisation strategy. "auto": Keep intersection branches. "NONPLS": Apply NONPLS filters . "PLS": Apply PLS filters . merge_replicates Logical. Average technical replicates. Default: FALSE. verbose Logical. Print progress. Default: TRUE.

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: Zero Handling: pipeline automatically converts 0 values x NA prior analysis. represents strong, domain-specific assumption (common mass spectrometry metabolomics) zeros signify structural missingness (e.g., values limit detection) rather true biological absence.

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) n_s <- 80; n_f <- 100 x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(n_s * n_f, 500, 150)), nrow = n_s, ncol = n_f) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", seq_len(n_f)) x[sample(length(x), 400)] <- 0  meta <- data.frame(   Sample          = paste0("S", seq_len(n_s)),   Group           = rep(c("Control", "Treatment", "QC"), c(30, 30, 20)),   Batch           = rep(1:2, each = 40),   InjectionSequence = seq_len(n_s),   SubjectID       = c(paste0("BIO", seq_len(60)), rep(NA, 20)),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) rownames(x) <- meta$Sample  result <- run_DIpreprocess(   x              = x,   metadata       = meta,   normalize_method = "pqn_group",   transform_method = "log2",   scale_nonpls   = "auto",   scale_pls      = "pareto",   correct_drift  = FALSE )  dim(result$data_nonpls) result$dimensions } # }

what: Compute WHO Anthropometric Z-Scores and Nutritional Status — run_anthroindex
title: Compute WHO Anthropometric Z-Scores and Nutritional Status — run_anthroindex
text: Computes Child Growth Standards z-scores eight anthropometric indices—weight--age (WFA), length/height--age (HFA), weight--length/height (WFH), BMI--age (BMIFA), head circumference--age (HCFA), arm circumference--age (ACFA), triceps skinfold--age (TSFA), subscapular skinfold--age (SSFA)—using anthro_zscores (Child Growth Standards 2006) ages 0 < 5 years, anthroplus_zscores (Growth Reference 2007) ages 5 < 20 years applicable. BMIFA, function covers full age range: 0 < 5 years: z-scores anthro_zscores, Child Growth Standards 2006. 5 < 20 years: z-scores anthroplus_zscores, Growth Reference 2007. anthroplus package must installed separately participants age range present. 20 years : nutritional status classified directly computed BMI value using adult cut-offs (z-score). Nutritional Status column appended result according classification thresholds.

title: Compute WHO Anthropometric Z-Scores and Nutritional Status — run_anthroindex
code: run_anthroindex(   x,   sex_col,   male_code = NULL,   female_code = NULL,   age_col,   age_unit = c("month", "day", "week", "year"),   weight_col = NULL,   weight_unit = c("kg", "g", "oz", "lb"),   height_col = NULL,   height_unit = c("cm", "m", "ft", "in"),   height_measured = "L",   headcircumference_col = NULL,   headcircumference_unit = c("cm", "m", "ft", "in"),   armcircumference_col = NULL,   armcircumference_unit = c("cm", "m", "ft", "in"),   tricepskinfold_col = NULL,   tricepskinfold_unit = c("mm", "cm", "m", "ft", "in"),   subscapularskinfold_col = NULL,   subscapularskinfold_unit = c("mm", "cm", "m", "ft", "in"),   oedema_col = NULL,   merge_obese = FALSE,   merge_wasted = merge_obese,   merge_malnourished = merge_obese,   merge_underweight = merge_obese,   merge_stunted = merge_obese,   merge_thinness = merge_obese,   status_col_name = "Nutritional Status",   simplify = TRUE,   return = c("wfa", "hfa", "wfh", "bmifa", "hcfa", "acfa", "tsfa", "ssfa") )

title: Compute WHO Anthropometric Z-Scores and Nutritional Status — run_anthroindex
text: x data.frame, tibble, named matrix containing anthropometric measurements. Column names may include special characters. sex_col single character string giving column name sex variable x. column may character, factor, numeric. See Details automatic manual coding rules. male_code Optional single character string indicating male participants coded sex_col. Required automatic detection fails. female_code Optional single character string indicating female participants coded sex_col. Required automatic detection fails. age_col single character string giving column name age variable x. age_unit single character string specifying unit age_col. One "day", "week", "month", "year". weight_col single character string giving column name weight variable x. weight_unit single character string specifying weight unit. One "kg" (default), "g", "oz", "lb". height_col single character string giving column name length/height variable x. height_unit single character string specifying height unit. One "cm" (default), "m", "ft", "". height_measured single valid measurement-mode code ("L", "l", "Length", "length" recumbent; "H", "h", "Height", "height" standing), character vector length nrow(x) giving mode row, name column x containing codes. headcircumference_col Optional single character string giving column name head circumference variable x. headcircumference_unit single character string specifying head circumference unit. One "cm" (default), "m", "ft", "". armcircumference_col Optional single character string giving column name mid-upper arm circumference (MUAC) variable x. armcircumference_unit single character string specifying MUAC unit. One "cm" (default), "m", "ft", "". tricepskinfold_col Optional single character string giving column name triceps skinfold variable x. tricepskinfold_unit single character string specifying triceps skinfold unit. One "mm" (default), "cm", "m", "ft", "". subscapularskinfold_col Optional single character string giving column name subscapular skinfold variable x. subscapularskinfold_unit single character string specifying subscapular skinfold unit. One "mm" (default), "cm", "m", "ft", "". oedema_col Optional single character string giving column name bilateral pitting oedema variable x. Valid cell values "n", "N", "2" (oedema) "y", "Y", "1" (oedema present). merge_obese Logical. TRUE, combines stages Obesity ("Stage 1 Obesity", "Stage 2 Obesity", "Stage 3 Obesity", "Obesity") just "Obese". Defaults FALSE. merge_wasted Logical. TRUE, combines categories wasted ("Severely wasted", "Moderately wasted") just "Wasted". Defaults value merge_obese. merge_malnourished Logical. TRUE, combines "Severe acute malnutrition" "Moderate acute malnutrition" just "Malnourished". Defaults value merge_obese. merge_underweight Logical. TRUE, combines "Severely underweight" "Moderately underweight" just "Underweight". Defaults value merge_obese. merge_stunted Logical. TRUE, combines "Severely stunted" "Moderately stunted" just "Stunted". Defaults value merge_obese. merge_thinness Logical. TRUE, combines categories thinness ("Severe thinness", "Thinness", "Moderate severe thinness") just "Thinness". Defaults value merge_obese. status_col_name single character string specifying name column created nutritional status classification. Defaults "Nutritional Status". simplify Logical. TRUE (default), excludes additional flag auxiliary z-score columns (e.g., fbmi, zbmia, sbmi) result, keeping supporting columns primary z_score. return character string vector specifying anthropometric indices compute. One : "wfa", "hfa", "wfh", "bmifa", "hcfa", "acfa", "tsfa", "ssfa". Defaults eight.

title: Compute WHO Anthropometric Z-Scores and Nutritional Status — run_anthroindex
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Compute WHO Anthropometric Z-Scores and Nutritional Status — run_anthroindex
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(pondeR)  if (requireNamespace("anthro", quietly = TRUE)) {    # --- Synthetic dataset (no external data file required) ----------------   set.seed(42)   n <- 50   children <- data.frame(     child_id = seq_len(n),     sex      = sample(c("Male", "Female"), n, replace = TRUE),     age_mo   = runif(n, 1, 60),     wt_kg    = runif(n, 3, 20),     ht_cm    = runif(n, 50, 115),     oedema   = sample(c("n", "y"), n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.95, 0.05))   )    # --- Multiple indices — returns a named list ---------------------------   res <- run_anthroindex(     x               = children,     sex_col         = "sex",     age_col         = "age_mo",     age_unit        = "month",     weight_col      = "wt_kg",     weight_unit     = "kg",     height_col      = "ht_cm",     height_unit     = "cm",     height_measured = "L",     oedema_col      = "oedema",     return          = c("wfa", "hfa", "wfh", "bmifa")   )    head(res$wfa[,  c("z_score", "Nutritional Status")])   head(res$bmifa[, c("cbmi", "z_score", "Nutritional Status")])    # --- Single index — returns a plain data frame -------------------------   wfa_only <- run_anthroindex(     x               = children,     sex_col         = "sex",     age_col         = "age_mo",     age_unit        = "month",     weight_col      = "wt_kg",     weight_unit     = "kg",     height_col      = "ht_cm",     height_unit     = "cm",     height_measured = "L",     return          = "wfa"   )   head(wfa_only)    # --- Mixed-age dataset spanning all three age brackets ----------------   if (requireNamespace("anthroplus", quietly = TRUE)) {     mixed <- data.frame(       sex    = c("Male", "Female", "Male"),       age_yr = c(3, 14, 25),       wt_kg  = c(14, 38.8, 80),       ht_cm  = c(95, 151.13, 175)     )     bmi_res <- run_anthroindex(       x               = mixed,       sex_col         = "sex",       age_col         = "age_yr",       age_unit        = "year",       weight_col      = "wt_kg",       height_col      = "ht_cm",       height_measured = "H",       return          = "bmifa"     )     bmi_res[, c("age_yr", "cbmi", "z_score", "Nutritional Status")]   } } } # }

what: Automatic Statistical Comparison for Categorical Variables — run_assoc
title: Automatic Statistical Comparison for Categorical Variables — run_assoc
text: Performs automatic comparison two categorical variables. intelligently selects appropriate statistical test (Chi-square, Fisher's Exact Test, McNemar's Test), calculates measures association, performs post-hoc tests appropriate.

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison for Categorical Variables — run_assoc
code: run_assoc(   x,   var1,   var2,   weight = NULL,   paired = FALSE,   force_categorical = FALSE,   test_type = c("auto", "chisq", "fisher"),   expected_freq_threshold = 5,   continuity_correction = TRUE,   fisher_simulate = FALSE,   test_alpha = 0.05,   calculate_association = TRUE,   perform_posthoc = TRUE,   p_adjust_method = "BH",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison for Categorical Variables — run_assoc
text: x data frame containing variables analysis. var1 character vector one column names x specifying categorical variable(s) test. variable var1 independently paired var2 test association. listed columns must categorical (factor character), unless force_categorical = TRUE. var2 character string specifying name single categorical variable (typically grouping outcome column). variable var1 compared variable. weight optional character string specifying name weight column. Use x frequency-aggregated format (e.g., .data.frame.table()). paired logical indicating whether observations paired (McNemar's test). Default FALSE. force_categorical Logical. TRUE, column var1 var2 already factor character coerced factor analysis. useful numeric codes represent categorical groups (e.g., 0/1, 1/2/3). warning issued coerced column. Default FALSE. test_type character string specifying test strategy independent samples. One "auto" (default), "chisq", "fisher". Ignored paired = TRUE. expected_freq_threshold minimum expected cell frequency justify using Chi-square test test_type = "auto". Default 5. continuity_correction logical indicating whether apply Yates' continuity correction 2x2 Chi-square McNemar's tests. Default TRUE. fisher_simulate logical indicating whether use Monte Carlo simulation Fisher's Exact Test p-values. Recommended tables larger 2x2. Default FALSE. test_alpha Significance level final statistical test. Default 0.05. calculate_association Logical. Whether calculate measures association (Cramer's V, Phi, Odds Ratio). Requires effectsize. Default TRUE. perform_posthoc Logical. Whether perform post-hoc pairwise Fisher's tests significant tables one binary dimension. Requires rstatix. Default TRUE. p_adjust_method P-value adjustment method post-hoc comparisons. See p.adjust. Default "BH". verbose logical indicating whether print detailed messages. Default TRUE.

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison for Categorical Variables — run_assoc
text: list class "run_assoc" containing: test_used Character string statistical test performed. test_result htest object selected test. contingency_table Observed contingency table data frame. expected_table Expected frequencies data frame (NULL Fisher's/McNemar's). residuals Pearson residuals data frame (NULL Fisher's/McNemar's). stdres Standardized residuals data frame (NULL Fisher's/McNemar's). Values beyond ±1.96 ±2.58 indicate cells contributing significance. association Data frame association metrics (metric, estimate, CI, magnitude, interpretation), NULL. posthoc_result Data frame post-hoc pairwise comparisons, NULL. assumptions Data frame summarising assumption check results. var1 Name row variable. var2 Name column variable. paired paired setting used. test_alpha significance level used. warnings Character vector warnings generated. parameters List parameters used analysis.

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison for Categorical Variables — run_assoc
text: Automatic Statistical Comparison Categorical Variables Test Family Selection: paired = TRUE, requires 2x2 table performs McNemar's Test. paired = FALSE, assumes independent samples builds contingency table. Test Selection Logic (test_type = "auto" independent samples): choice Chi-square Fisher's test based Cochran's rule. function first calculates expected frequencies via Chi-square check. cell expected frequency expected_freq_threshold (default 5), Fisher's Exact Test used. Otherwise, Pearson's Chi-square Test used. Post-Hoc Tests: significant result table one dimension binary (2 x C R x 2), pairwise Fisher's tests performed via rstatix::pairwise_fisher_test. P-values adjusted using p_adjust_method. Measures Association (Effect Sizes): Phi Odds Ratio 2x2 independent tables. Cramer's V RxC independent tables (larger 2x2). Odds Ratio (paired) 2x2 paired (McNemar's) tables.

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison for Categorical Variables — run_assoc
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison for Categorical Variables — run_assoc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # --- Example 1: 2x2 Independent Test (Auto -> Chi-square) --- data(mtcars) res <- run_assoc(x = mtcars, var1 = "am", var2 = "vs",                  force_categorical = TRUE) print(res) summary(res)  # --- Example 2: RxC Independent Test --- data(HairEyeColor) he <- as.data.frame.table(HairEyeColor) he_male <- he[he$Sex == "Male", ] res_hair <- run_assoc(x = he_male, var1 = "Hair", var2 = "Eye", weight = "Freq") summary(res_hair)  # --- Example 3: Paired 2x2 (McNemar's) --- set.seed(42) N <- 100 before <- sample(c("No", "Yes"), N, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.7, 0.3)) after  <- before after[before == "No"][1:14]  <- "Yes" after[before == "Yes"][1:3]  <- "No" paired_df <- data.frame(before = before, after = after) res_paired <- run_assoc(x = paired_df, var1 = "before", var2 = "after",                          paired = TRUE) summary(res_paired)  # --- Example 4: Force Chi-square --- res_chisq <- run_assoc(x = mtcars, var1 = "am", var2 = "vs",                         test_type = "chisq", continuity_correction = TRUE,                         force_categorical = TRUE) print(res_chisq) } # }

what: Comprehensive Area Under the ROC Curve (AUROC) Analysis — run_auc
title: Comprehensive Area Under the ROC Curve (AUROC) Analysis — run_auc
text: Performs comprehensive Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) analysis one continuous predictors binary response variable. Wraps pROC::roc() compute AUC confidence intervals, optionally produces publication-ready ROC plots via ggplot2.

title: Comprehensive Area Under the ROC Curve (AUROC) Analysis — run_auc
code: run_auc(   x,   y = NULL,   remove = NULL,   metadata = NULL,   set_control = NULL,   include_cols = NULL,   include_rows = NULL,   direction = "auto",   compute_auc = TRUE,   compute_ci = TRUE,   ci_level = 0.95,   plot = "none",   theme = "nature",   ncol = NULL,   nrow = NULL,   plot_title = NULL,   plot_subtitle = NULL,   plot_legend = "AUC (95% CI)",   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   linewidth = 3,   global_font_size = 25,   title_font_size = NULL,   subtitle_font_size = NULL,   xlab_font_size = NULL,   ylab_font_size = NULL,   inplot_font_size = NULL,   min_vip = 1,   up = 1.5,   down = 0.6,   p_value = 0.05,   ... )

title: Comprehensive Area Under the ROC Curve (AUROC) Analysis — run_auc
text: x data.frame, tibble, named matrix, run_DIpreprocess object, list objects run_* ecosystem. list provided, MUST contain run_DIpreprocess object. Optional inclusion run_pls, run_foldchange, /run_diff objects trigger automatic feature filtering based min_vip, , , p_value thresholds. y single character string naming column x contains binary grouping variable (cases vs. controls). column may categorical (character / factor) exactly two levels optional removal via remove, numeric containing 0 (control) 1 (case) optional removal. x run_DIpreprocess object list containing one, y default x$parameters$group_col explicitly provided. remove Optional. vector values remove y analysis, used reduce multi-level variable exactly two. x run_DIpreprocess object list containing one, remove default x$parameters$qc_types explicitly provided. May character vector (categorical y), groups. May character vector (categorical y), numeric vector (numeric y), mixed list. Ignored y already contains exactly two categories 0/1 values. Default NULL. metadata optional object class row count x carrying additional sample annotations. Currently reserved future use ignored analysis. Default NULL. set_control single string specifying level categorical y represents control group. Ignored y numeric. Default NULL (first level alphabetically factor order). include_cols character vector column names, integer vector column positions, selecting predictor columns x analysed. Default NULL includes non-y columns. include_rows character vector row names, integer vector row positions, selecting rows x analysed. Row names considered rownames(x) default integer sequence. Default NULL includes rows. direction single string controlling direction comparison passed pROC::roc(). Choices: "auto" (default), ">" (controls > cases), "<" (controls < cases). See ?pROC::roc details. compute_auc Logical. Whether compute AUC. Default TRUE. compute_ci Logical. Whether compute bootstrap DeLong confidence intervals AUC. Default TRUE. ci_level numeric scalar \((0, 1)\) specifying confidence level. Default 0.95. plot Controls ROC plot generation. Accepted values: "none" plots produced. "" selected predictors overlaid one plot. "separate" One plot per predictor, returned named list. Integer \(\ge 1\) Plot top n predictors (AUC) one combined chart. Numeric \([0, 1)\) Plot predictors \(AUC \ge\) threshold. c("", n) c("separate", n) Combine string mode integer numeric filter described . Default "none". theme single string specifying ggplot2 theme plots. Supported values: "nature" (default), "minimal", "bw", "classic", "plos", "gray", "light", "dark". Colour-blind-friendly palettes applied automatically. ncol Integer. Number legend columns plot = "" plot = c("", ...). Default NULL (auto). nrow Integer. Number legend rows plot = "" plot = c("", ...). Default NULL (auto). plot_title character vector plot titles. NULL (default) uses "ROC Curve" combined plots "ROC Curve <predictor>" separate plots. plot = "separate", vector must match number predictors plotted. plot_subtitle single string used plot subtitle. Default NULL (subtitle). plot_legend single string legend title. Default "AUC (95% CI)". xlab single string x-axis label. Default NULL renders "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)". ylab single string y-axis label. Default NULL renders "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)". linewidth positive numeric value controlling ROC curve line width. Default 3. global_font_size positive numeric value setting base font size plot text elements. Default 25. Derived sizes: title \(= 1.20 \times\), subtitle \(= 0.90 \times\), axis labels / ticks \(= 1.00 \times\). title_font_size Override title font size. Default NULL (derived global_font_size). subtitle_font_size Override subtitle font size. Default NULL (derived global_font_size). xlab_font_size Override x-axis label tick font size. Default NULL (derived global_font_size). ylab_font_size Override y-axis label tick font size. Default NULL (derived global_font_size). inplot_font_size Font size -plot legend text (e.g., AUC labels combined plots). Default NULL (derived global_font_size). min_vip single numeric value specifying minimum VIP score features considered. applied run_pls object provided x list. Default 1.0. single numeric value specifying minimum fold change -regulated features. applied run_foldchange object provided x list. Default params$. x data.frame, tibble, named matrix, default 1.5. single numeric value specifying maximum fold change -regulated features. applied run_foldchange object provided x list. Default params$. x data.frame, tibble, named matrix, default 0.6. p_value single numeric value specifying maximum acceptable p-value features. applied run_diff object provided x list. Default 0.05. ... Additional arguments forwarded pROC::roc(), pROC::auc(), pROC::ci().

title: Comprehensive Area Under the ROC Curve (AUROC) Analysis — run_auc
text: named list following elements: roc_list Named list pROC::roc objects, one per analysed predictor. auc_table data.frame columns predictor, auc, ci_lower, ci_upper, ci_level, direction, n_cases / n_controls. plots named list ggplot objects (NULL plot = "none"). element "combined" present combined-mode plots; individual predictor names used keys separate-mode plots. params list echoing validated call parameters reproducibility.

title: Comprehensive Area Under the ROC Curve (AUROC) Analysis — run_auc
text: Workflow overview x coerced data.frame; columns rows subset according include_cols / include_rows. y values listed remove dropped, leaving exactly two groups. y categorical, two remaining levels mapped 0 (control, specified set_control) 1 (case). y already encodes 0/1 character factor, silently converted numeric. pROC::roc() called predictor column; AUC , optionally, DeLong confidence intervals (pROC::ci()) extracted. Plots assembled ggplot2 using colour-blind-friendly palette (Okabe–Ito). AUC labels rounded two decimal places; values round 1.00 progressively displayed decimal places (five), scientific notation two significant figures used. Reference line grey dashed diagonal line representing AUC 0.5 (random classifier) always drawn. Legend placement combined plots (plot = "") legend positioned bottom-right inside plot area default. pROC dependency function requires pROC (\(\ge\) 1.18) ggplot2 (\(\ge\) 3.4). listed Imports package DESCRIPTION file.

title: Comprehensive Area Under the ROC Curve (AUROC) Analysis — run_auc
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Comprehensive Area Under the ROC Curve (AUROC) Analysis — run_auc
code: ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 1 — numeric binary response (0/1), all predictors ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- set.seed(42) n  <- 120 df <- data.frame(   group    = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),   marker_A = rnorm(n, mean = ifelse(sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE) == 1, 1, 0)),   marker_B = rnorm(n),   marker_C = runif(n) )  res <- run_auc(x = df, y = "group", plot = "none") print(res$auc_table) #>   predictor       auc  ci_lower  ci_upper ci_level direction n_cases n_controls #> 1  marker_A 0.5133765 0.4085016 0.6182514     0.95         >      67         53 #> 2  marker_B 0.5243593 0.4185301 0.6301886     0.95         <      67         53 #> 3  marker_C 0.5350605 0.4300641 0.6400569     0.95         >      67         53  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 2 — categorical response, remove one group, combined plot ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- df2 <- data.frame(   status   = sample(c("control", "case", "unknown"), n, replace = TRUE),   score_1  = rnorm(n),   score_2  = rnorm(n) )  res2 <- run_auc(   x           = df2,   y           = "status",   remove      = "unknown",   set_control = "control",   plot        = "none",   compute_ci  = FALSE ) #> Moving non-numeric columns to metadata (predictors must be numeric): status print(res2$auc_table) #>   predictor       auc ci_lower ci_upper ci_level direction n_cases n_controls #> 1   score_1 0.5257453       NA       NA     0.95         >      36         41 #> 2   score_2 0.5372629       NA       NA     0.95         <      36         41  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 3 — column names with spaces, select top-2 by AUC ## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- df3 <- data.frame(   `my group`  = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),   `feature 1` = rnorm(n, mean = 0.5),   `feature 2` = rnorm(n),   check.names = FALSE )  res3 <- run_auc(   x    = df3,   y    = "my group",   plot = "none" ) print(res3$auc_table) #>   predictor       auc  ci_lower  ci_upper ci_level direction n_cases n_controls #> 1 feature 1 0.5167558 0.4120382 0.6214735     0.95         >      67         53 #> 2 feature 2 0.5516756 0.4461648 0.6571863     0.95         >      67         53

title: Perform Correlation Analysis — run_correl
text: Computes pairwise targeted correlation analysis numeric, ordinal, binary variables data frame, tibble, matrix. Supports automatic method selection (Pearson, Spearman, Kendall, point-biserial, phi, Cramer's V, polychoric, polyserial) based data characteristics. Returns tidy output tables correlation estimates, masked correlations, confidence intervals, p-values.

title: Perform Correlation Analysis — run_correl
code: run_correl(   x,   y = NULL,   z = NULL,   metadata = NULL,   remove = NULL,   method = "auto",   force_test = NULL,   sig_threshold = 0.05,   normality_method = "auto",   alpha_normality = 0.05,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Perform Correlation Analysis — run_correl
text: x data frame, tibble, matrix whose columns variables correlate. Column names may contain special characters. y character vector naming one columns x use primary variables. y z NULL, pairwise correlations among numeric columns x computed. y supplied, correlations among variables y y columns returned. z single character string naming second column x. supplied together y, correlations element y z computed. metadata data frame number rows x containing sample-level metadata. Appended returned tidy tables provided used correlation computation. remove named list one-sided formulas form list("colname" ~ c("level1", "level2")) specifying levels column x exclude analysis. left-hand side must single column name; right-hand side may character vector one levels. method single character string specifying correlation method. One "auto" (default), "pearson", "spearman", "kendall", "pointbiserial", "phi", "cramerv", "poly". "auto", method selected per variable pair based data type, normality, sample size. force_test list two-element lists, specifying variable pair correlation method use pair, overriding global method argument. element must form list(c("var1", "var2"), "method"). example: Valid methods values accepted method except "auto". Column names order-insensitive within pair. pair appears , last entry takes precedence warning. Pairs listed analyzed using global method. Default NULL (overrides). sig_threshold single numeric value (0, 1) specifying significance threshold p-values. Correlations p >= sig_threshold masked masked table. Default 0.05. normality_method single character string, one "auto" (default), "shapiro", "lilliefors", controlling normality test used automatic method selection. "auto" applies Shapiro-Wilk \(n < 50\) Lilliefors otherwise. alpha_normality single numeric value (0, 1) specifying significance threshold used internally normality variance tests method = "auto". Default 0.05. verbose Logical. TRUE, diagnostic messages assumption checks method selection printed. Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed cor.test.
code: force_test = list(     list(c("var1", "var2"), "pearson"),     list(c("var1", "var3"), "spearman")   )

title: Perform Correlation Analysis — run_correl
text: named list class "pondeR_correl" following elements: correlations tidy data frame pairwise correlation estimates columns var1, var2, estimate, method_used, n. correlations_masked correlations estimate NA pairs p_value >= sig_threshold. p_values tidy data frame columns var1, var2, p_value. confidence_intervals tidy data frame columns var1, var2, ci_lower, ci_upper. sig_threshold significance threshold used. method method argument supplied. call matched call.

title: Perform Correlation Analysis — run_correl
text: Automatic method selection (method = "auto"): Normality assessed via Shapiro-Wilk (\(n < 50\)) Lilliefors (\(n \ge 50\); requires nortest). Homoscedasticity assessed via Levene's test (requires car) Bartlett's test fallback. Confidence intervals Cramer's V polychoric/polyserial correlations available returned NA.

title: Perform Correlation Analysis — run_correl
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

what: Automatic Statistical Comparison with Comprehensive Analysis — run_diff
title: Automatic Statistical Comparison with Comprehensive Analysis — run_diff
text: Performs automatic comparison numeric outcome variable across two groups. Intelligently selects appropriate statistical test (parametric non-parametric) based checks normality (data residuals) homogeneity variances, calculates effect sizes, performs post-hoc tests appropriate. outcome character vector length > 1 summary_table = TRUE, additional $summary_table element returned alongside per-outcome list. subgroup also specified summary_table = TRUE, additional named elements $summary_table_<subgroup_var> (one per subgroup variable) appended; named list per-level summary tables, e.g. $summary_table_Gender$Male.

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison with Comprehensive Analysis — run_diff
code: run_diff(   x,   metadata = NULL,   outcome,   group = NULL,   filter = NULL,   within = NULL,   subject_id = NULL,   paired = FALSE,   test_type = c("auto", "parametric", "nonparametric"),   normality_method = c("auto", "shapiro", "lilliefors"),   alpha_normality = 0.05,   alpha_variance = 0.05,   alpha_sphericity = 0.05,   type_sosquares = 2,   rm_effect_size = "ges",   correction = "auto",   test_alpha = 0.05,   min_n_threshold = 3,   calculate_effect_size = TRUE,   perform_posthoc = TRUE,   p_adjust_method = "BH",   group_order = NULL,   subgroup = NULL,   summary_table = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   num_cores = 1 )

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison with Comprehensive Analysis — run_diff
text: x data frame, object class "run_DIpreprocess". data frame, contain numeric features/metabolites analysed. metadata data frame containing sample-level metadata (e.g., grouping, subgroups, subject IDs). Required x data frame. outcome character string character vector naming numeric outcome variable(s) found x. group character string naming grouping variable found metadata. May NULL pure within-subjects designs. filter Optional character vector levels group exclude analysis. x run_DIpreprocess object, defaults identified QC types. within Character vector within-subject factor column name(s) found metadata. supplied, subject_id must also provided. Triggers repeated-measures mixed-ANOVA logic. subject_id Character string naming subject identifier column found metadata. Required within non-NULL. paired Logical; whether observations paired. Default FALSE. test_type One "auto" (default), "parametric", "nonparametric". normality_method One "auto" (default), "shapiro", "lilliefors". alpha_normality Significance level normality tests. Default 0.05. alpha_variance Significance level variance-homogeneity test. Default 0.05. alpha_sphericity Significance level Mauchly's test. Default 0.05. type_sosquares Integer (1, 2, 3) specifying sums--squares type RM / mixed ANOVA. Default 2. rm_effect_size Character vector; one "ges" (generalized eta-squared, default) "pes" (partial eta-squared). Generalized eta-squared default directly comparable across designs differ number within- -subject factors. Use "pes" matching output software SPSS. Specify c("ges", "pes") compute ; first listed metric treated primary. Applies RM / mixed ANOVA designs. correction Sphericity correction: "auto" (default; applies Greenhouse-Geisser sphericity violated), "GG", "HF", "none". Applies RM / mixed ANOVA. test_alpha Significance level final test. Default 0.05. min_n_threshold Minimum per-group sample size. Default 3. calculate_effect_size Logical. Default TRUE. perform_posthoc Logical. Default TRUE. p_adjust_method P-value adjustment method post-hoc comparisons. Default "BH". group_order Optional character vector specifying group level order. subgroup Optional character vector column names metadata stratified analysis. summary_table Logical; outcome length > 1 TRUE, appends $summary_table (subgroup summary tables) returned list. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Default TRUE. num_cores Integer "max". Default 1.

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison with Comprehensive Analysis — run_diff
text: outcome single string, list class "run_diff" containing: test_used, test_result, effect_size, posthoc_result, assumptions, parametric, data_summary, outcome, group_var, within_vars (RM ), subject_id_var (RM ), anova_table (RM ), sphericity (RM ), sphericity_corrections (RM ), paired, test_type, test_alpha, subgroup_analysis, warnings, parameters, raw_data. outcome length > 1, named list "run_diff" objects, optionally $summary_table $summary_table_<sg> elements.

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison with Comprehensive Analysis — run_diff
text: Automatic Statistical Comparison Comprehensive Analysis Test Family Selection: paired = FALSE, independent groups compared. 2 groups independent paired two-sample test chosen; > 2 groups ANOVA-family test used. Assumption Checking (test_type = "auto"): Normality: Shapiro-Wilk (n < 50) Lilliefors (n ≥ 50) "auto". Per-group 2-group comparisons; model residuals > 2 groups. Homogeneity Variance: Levene's test (car); falls back Bartlett's test car unavailable. Test Selection Logic (test_type = "auto"): Independent two-sample: Mann-Whitney U (normality violated) → Welch's t (variance violated) → Student's t. Paired two-sample: Wilcoxon signed-rank (normality violated) → Paired t. Independent > 2 groups: Kruskal-Wallis (normality violated) → Welch's ANOVA (variance violated) → One-way ANOVA. Post-Hoc Tests (> 2 groups, significant omnibus): Tukey HSD (ANOVA), Games-Howell (Welch's ANOVA), Dunn's test (Kruskal-Wallis), Pairwise t-test (RM / Mixed ANOVA). Effect Sizes: Cohen's d / paired Cohen's d (t-tests), rank-biserial correlation (Wilcoxon/Mann-Whitney), eta-squared (ANOVA), epsilon-squared (Kruskal-Wallis), generalized partial eta-squared (RM / Mixed ANOVA). Non-Parametric Interpretation (Stochastic Dominance): non-parametric tests interpretation column reads "X tends larger/smaller values Y".

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison with Comprehensive Analysis — run_diff
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Automatic Statistical Comparison with Comprehensive Analysis — run_diff

what: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: Applies Quality Control-based Robust Spline Correction (QCRSC) batch correction using ComBat remove systematic signal drift batch effects metabolomics data.

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
code: run_driftBatchCorrect(   x,   metadata,   perform_correction = TRUE,   batch_corr_only = FALSE,   injection_sequence = "InjectionSequence",   batch_numbers = "Batches",   groups = "Group",   qc_label = "QC",   qc_types = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   spline_smooth_param = 0,   min_QC = 5,   spar_limit = c(-1.5, 1.5),   log_scale = TRUE,   use_parametric = TRUE,   display_plots = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: x Matrix data frame. Numeric data samples rows features columns. metadata Data frame. Sample metadata number rows equal nrow(x). Must contain columns specified injection_sequence, batch_numbers, groups. perform_correction Logical. TRUE, perform correction; FALSE, return original data unchanged. Default: TRUE. batch_corr_only Logical. TRUE, perform batch correction using ComBat (drift correction). FALSE, perform QCRSC (drift + batch correction). Default: FALSE. injection_sequence Character. Name column metadata containing injection order. Required QCRSC. Default: "InjectionSequence". batch_numbers Character. Name column metadata containing batch numbers. Required QCRSC ComBat. Default: "Batches". groups Character. Name column metadata containing sample group labels (e.g., "Sample", "QC", "SQC", "EQC"). Required QCRSC. Default: "Group". qc_label Character. Group label identifying QC samples QCRSC. Default: "QC". qc_types Character vector. Group labels converted qc_label internally. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). spline_smooth_param Numeric. Smoothing parameter spline fitting QCRSC (0 = flexible, 1 = rigid). Default: 0. min_QC Integer. Minimum number QC samples required per batch QCRSC. Batches fewer QCs corrected. Default: 5. spar_limit Numeric vector length 2. Limits spline fitting QCRSC c(min, max). Default: c(-1.5, 1.5). log_scale Logical. TRUE, fit QCRSC spline log-transformed data. Default: TRUE. use_parametric Logical. ComBat . Use parametric adjustment batch effects. Default: TRUE. display_plots Logical. ComBat . Display diagnostic plots correction. Default: FALSE. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: list containing: data Matrix data frame corrected data (class input x) data_before_correction Original data correction correction_applied Logical indicating correction performed method_used Character describing correction method ("QCRSC" "ComBat") uncorrected_features Character vector features corrected n_uncorrected Integer. Number uncorrected features parameters List parameters used

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: QCRSC (Quality Control-based Robust Spline Correction): QCRSC default recommended method metabolomics data regular QC injections. works : Fitting smoothing spline QC sample intensities across injection order Using spline model systematic drift batch effects Normalizing samples (biological + QC) remove fitted drift use QCRSC: Long analytical runs visible signal drift Multiple batches sufficient QC samples (≥ min_QC per batch) QC samples show systematic trends across injection order ComBat Batch Correction: batch_corr_only = TRUE, uses ComBat algorithm batch effect removal (drift correction). useful : QC samples insufficient absent Primary concern batch differences, within-batch drift Data comes multiple platforms sites ComBat uses empirical Bayes methods adjust batch effects preserving biological variation. Uncorrected Features: features may remain uncorrected due : Insufficient QC samples (< min_QC) one batches Poor spline fit numerical issues QC values identical (variance model) Uncorrected features returned unchanged names listed output.

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: Kirwan, J.., Broadhurst, D.., Davidson, R.L. et al. (2013). Characterising correcting batch variation automated direct infusion mass spectrometry (DIMS) metabolomics workflow. Analytical Bioanalytical Chemistry, 405, 5147-5157. doi:10.1007/s00216-013-6856-7 Johnson, W.E., Li, C., & Rabinovic, . (2007). Adjusting batch effects microarray expression data using empirical Bayes methods. Biostatistics, 8(1), 118-127. doi:10.1093/biostatistics/kxj037 Jankevics , Lloyd GR, Weber RJM (2025). pmp: Peak Matrix Processing signal batch correction metabolomics datasets. doi:10.18129/B9.bioc.pmp , R package version 1.20.0, https://bioconductor.org/packages/pmp.

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simulate data with drift and batch effects set.seed(123) n_samples <- 120 n_features <- 50 x <- matrix(rnorm(n_samples * n_features, mean = 100, sd = 20),             nrow = n_samples, ncol = n_features) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:n_features)  # Add systematic drift drift <- seq(0, 30, length.out = n_samples) x <- x + drift  metadata <- data.frame(   Sample = paste0("S", 1:n_samples),   InjectionSequence = 1:n_samples,   Batches = rep(1:3, each = 40),   Group = rep(c(rep("Sample", 9), "QC"), n_samples / 10) )  # Apply QCRSC correction result1 <- run_driftBatchCorrect(x, metadata)  # Apply ComBat correction only result2 <- run_driftBatchCorrect(x, metadata, batch_corr_only = TRUE) } # }

what: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: Removes features high relative standard deviation (RSD, also known coefficient variation) quality control samples. High RSD indicates poor analytical reproducibility.

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
code: run_filterRSD(   x,   metadata,   max_rsd = 0.3,   groups = "Group",   qc_type = "EQC",   qc_types = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: x Matrix data frame. Numeric data samples rows features columns. metadata Data frame. Sample metadata number rows equal nrow(x). Must contain column specified groups identify QC samples. max_rsd Numeric. Maximum allowed RSD (0-1). Features RSD >= threshold QC samples removed. Default: 0.3 (30%). groups Character. Name column metadata containing sample group labels. Default: "Group". qc_type Character. QC sample type use RSD calculation. Options: "EQC": Extract QC samples (diluted sample pool) "SQC": Sample QC samples (undiluted sample pool) "QC": QC sample (use EQC SQC present, distinction) Default: "EQC". qc_types Character vector. Group labels treated QC samples. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: list containing: data Matrix data frame filtered data (class input x) features_removed Character vector removed feature names features_kept Character vector retained feature names rsd_values Numeric vector RSD values original features n_features_before Integer. Number features filtering n_features_after Integer. Number features filtering n_features_removed Integer. Number features removed parameters List parameters used

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: Relative Standard Deviation (RSD): RSD calculated : RSD = (SD / Mean) × 100% Also known coefficient variation (CV), RSD expresses variability percentage mean, making scale-independent suitable comparing features different magnitudes. Filter RSD: Quality control samples low variability since represent biological matrix. High RSD QC samples indicates: Poor analytical reproducibility Instrument instability Ion suppression/enhancement effects Features near detection limit Features high QC RSD unreliable removed biological interpretation. Choosing QC Type: EQC (default): commonly used. Represents diluted pooled samples analyzed regularly throughout run. SQC: Undiluted pooled samples. Use specific SQC type distinct EQC. QC: Use distinguish QC types, want assess RSD across QC samples combined. Typical RSD Thresholds: Stringent: 20% (0.2) - high-quality data Standard: 30% (0.3) - recommended metabolomics studies Lenient: 40% (0.4) - sample size limited

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD

Retrieved https://github.com/broadhurstdavid/QC-MXP. title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simulate data set.seed(123) x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100, sd = 20)),             nrow = 100, ncol = 50) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50)  # Add high variability to some features in QC samples qc_rows <- 91:100 x[qc_rows, 1:10] <- x[qc_rows, 1:10] * runif(10 * 10, 0.5, 1.5)  metadata <- data.frame(   Sample = paste0("S", 1:100),   Group = c(rep("Control", 40), rep("Treatment", 40), rep("QC", 10), rep("EQC", 10)) )  # Filter by RSD in EQC samples result <- run_filterRSD(x, metadata, max_rsd = 0.3, qc_type = "EQC") } # }

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
text: function filters features based proportion missing values (NAs). Zero values treated missing metabolomics data, zero values often represent one following: Peak intensities detection limit Failed peak integration Metabolites detected sample zero_as_missing = TRUE (default), zero values converted missing values filtering. recommended untargeted metabolomics datasets. Set zero_as_missing = FALSE one following true: preprocessing pipeline already converted zeros NA Zero represents true biological absence quantitative meaning dataset contains negative values (example baseline correction) Filtering strategies Two filtering modes available: Global filtering (filter_by_group = FALSE): Missingness calculated across eligible samples. Features missing proportion greater equal threshold removed. Group-wise filtering (filter_by_group = TRUE): Missingness calculated separately within group. feature retained satisfies threshold least one group. approach less stringent helps preserve group-specific features. QC sample handling include_QC = FALSE, samples whose group labels match qc_types excluded missingness calculations. recommended QC samples often exhibit different missingness patterns compared biological samples.

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simulate data with zeros and NAs set.seed(123) x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100)), nrow = 100, ncol = 50) x[sample(length(x), 300)] <- 0    # Add zeros x[sample(length(x), 200)] <- NA   # Add NAs colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50)  metadata <- data.frame(   Sample = paste0("S", 1:100),   Group = rep(c("Control", "Treatment", "QC"), c(40, 40, 20)) )  # Treat zeros as missing (default) result1 <- run_filtermissing(x, metadata, threshold = 0.3)  # Keep zeros as valid values result2 <- run_filtermissing(x, metadata, threshold = 0.3,                                zero_as_missing = FALSE)  # Group-wise filtering with zeros as missing result3 <- run_filtermissing(x, metadata, threshold = 0.3,                                filter_by_group = TRUE,                               zero_as_missing = TRUE) } # }
