title: Scale Metabolomics Data — run_scale
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simulate data set.seed(123) x <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100, sd = 50),             nrow = 100, ncol = 50) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50)  # Auto-scaling for PCA result1 <- run_scale(x, method = "auto")  # Pareto-scaling for PLS-DA result2 <- run_scale(x, method = "pareto")  # Mean-centering only result3 <- run_scale(x, method = "mean") } # }

what: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
text: Generates comprehensive descriptive statistics table given dataframe, optionally stratified categorical variable. Provides customisable summaries continuous categorical variables, including control rounding, missing data reporting, labelling, formatting, table aesthetics. output publication-ready gtsummary / gt table. add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE, p-values computed outside gtsummary using run_diff (continuous variables) run_assoc (categorical variables), injected table. p-value cell carries letter superscript identifying exact test used; legend appended table footnotes. Variables p-value computed shown ---† corresponding footnote.

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
code: run_summarytable(   x,   summarize_what = NULL,   split_by = NULL,   filter = NULL,   split_by_header = NULL,   strata_by = NULL,   rename_variables = NULL,   continuous_statistics = "meanSD",   categorical_statistics = "n_percent",   force_continuous = NULL,   force_categorical = NULL,   n_digits_continuous = c(2, 2),   n_digits_categorical = c(0, 2),   zero_as_exp = TRUE,   display_missing = "ifany",   missing_text = "No data/missing",   missing_stat = "n_percent",   include_missing_in_splits = FALSE,   sort_categorical_variables_by = "alphanumeric",   calc_percent_by = "column",   calc_col_percent_using = "n_valid_in_column",   add_inferential_pvalues = FALSE,   test_type_continuous = c("auto", "parametric", "nonparametric"),   test_type_categorical = c("auto", "chisq", "fisher"),   paired = FALSE,   n_digits_pvalues = 3,   bold_significant_pvalues = TRUE,   bold_significant_pvalues_at = 0.05,   header = "Variable",   show_n_header = TRUE,   bold_labels = TRUE,   italicize_levels = TRUE,   clean_table = TRUE,   table_name = "auto" )

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
text: x Dataframe. input dataframe. summarize_what Character vector. Column names include analysis. NULL (default), columns except named split_by strata_by included. split_by String. column name x (must categorical least 2 unique levels) stratify table. Required add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE. Default NULL. filter Optional character vector levels split_by exclude analysis. x run_DIpreprocess object, defaults identified QC types. split_by_header String. Display header split_by variable. Defaults value split_by. strata_by String. column name x (must categorical least 2 unique levels) stratify split_by. combined add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE. Default NULL. rename_variables List. Formulas form list("original" ~ "new", ...) used relabel variables table. continuous_statistics String. Summary statistic(s) continuous variables. One : "meanSD", "meanSD2", "medianIQR", "mean", "sd", "median", "p0", "p25", "p50", "p75", "p100", "IQR". Default "meanSD". categorical_statistics String. Summary statistic(s) categorical variables. One : "n_percent", "n", "percent". Default "n_percent". force_continuous Character vector. Column names treat continuous regardless storage type. coercion applied passing data run_diff. force_categorical Character vector. Column names treat categorical regardless storage type. coercion applied passing data run_assoc. n_digits_continuous Numeric vector c(d1, d2) "dynamic". Number decimal places first subsequent numeric tokens continuous summary cells (e.g., mean SD). Default c(2, 2). n_digits_categorical Numeric vector c(d1, d2) "dynamic". Decimal places count percentage categorical summary cells. Default c(0, 2). zero_as_exp Logical. TRUE (default), continuous summary tokens round 0 requested precision genuinely non-zero displayed scientific notation (e.g., 1.00e-3). display_missing String. report missing values: "ifany" (default), "", "always". missing_text String. Label missing-value rows. Default "data/missing". missing_stat String. Format missing-value counts: one "n_percent" (default), "n", "percent". include_missing_in_splits Logical. TRUE, missing values split_by / strata_by columns kept explicit level. Defaults FALSE. sort_categorical_variables_by String. Level ordering categorical variables: "alphanumeric" (default) "frequency". calc_percent_by String. Percentage base categorical variables: "column" (default), "row", "cell", "total". "total" calculates percentages based total number samples, study participants, simply number rows x. calc_col_percent_using String. Denominator calc_percent_by = "column": "n_valid_in_column" (default) "n_in_column". add_inferential_pvalues Logical. TRUE, p-values computed via run_diff (continuous variables) run_assoc (categorical variables) injected table. Requires split_by non-NULL strata_by NULL. Default FALSE. test_type_continuous String. Test strategy forwarded run_diff add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE. One "auto" (default), "parametric", "nonparametric". See run_diff details. test_type_categorical String. Test strategy forwarded run_assoc add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE. One "auto" (default), "chisq", "fisher". See run_assoc details. paired Logical. TRUE, paired tests used computing p-values. Forwarded run_diff run_assoc. Note descriptive statistics shown table body always unpaired summaries. Default FALSE. n_digits_pvalues Numeric NULL. Decimal places displayed p-values. Default 3. Set NULL automatic formatting (3 d.p. \(p < 0.10\), otherwise 1 d.p.). bold_significant_pvalues Logical. TRUE (default), p-values bold_significant_pvalues_at bolded. bold_significant_pvalues_at Numeric. Significance threshold bolding. Default 0.05. header String. Header variable-label column. Default "Variable". show_n_header Logical. TRUE (default), appends total sample size header (e.g., "Variable (N = 100)"). bold_labels Logical. TRUE (default), variable labels bolded. italicize_levels Logical. TRUE (default), categorical level labels italicised. clean_table Logical. TRUE (default), cells containing zero count (e.g., 0 (0.00%)) replaced empty strings. table_name String NULL. Table title. "auto" (default) generates title automatically. NULL suppresses title.

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
text: gt_tbl object (via gtsummary::as_gt()) representing descriptive (optionally inferential) statistics table.

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(gtsummary) library(dplyr)  set.seed(123) sample_df <- data.frame(   Age       = sample(c(18:65, NA), 100, replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(rep(1, 48), 5)),   Gender    = sample(c("Male", "Female", NA), 100, replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(0.48, 0.48, 0.04)),   Education = sample(c("Elementary", "High School",                         "Bachelors", "Masters", NA), 100,                      replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(0.40, 0.30, 0.15, 0.10, 0.05)),   Income    = sample(c(seq(5000, 40000, by = 5000), NA), 100,                      replace = TRUE, prob = c(rep(1, 8), 2)),   Smoker    = sample(c(1, 0, NA), 100, replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(0.25, 0.65, 0.10)),   Region    = sample(c("North", "South", "East", "West", NA), 100,                      replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(0.24, 0.24, 0.24, 0.24, .04)) )  # Basic descriptive statistics (no grouping) sample_df |> run_summarytable()  # Descriptive statistics split by Gender sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     split_by        = "Gender",     split_by_header = "Participant Gender",     rename_variables = list(Age    ~ "Age (Years)",                             Income ~ "Annual Income",                             Smoker ~ "Smoking Status")   )  # Median and IQR for continuous variables sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     continuous_statistics  = "medianIQR",     categorical_statistics = "n",     n_digits_continuous    = c(0, 0),     n_digits_categorical   = c(0, 0)   )  # Inferential p-values using run_diff (continuous) and run_assoc # (categorical), auto test selection sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     split_by                = "Gender",     add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE   )  # Force non-parametric tests for continuous variables sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     split_by                = "Gender",     add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE,     test_type_continuous    = "nonparametric",     test_type_categorical   = "fisher"   )  # Paired inferential p-values (descriptive body remains unpaired) sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     split_by                = "Gender",     add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE,     paired                  = TRUE   ) } # }

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: Applies various transformation methods stabilize variance reduce heteroscedasticity metabolomics data.

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
code: run_transform(   x,   method = "log2",   metadata = NULL,   groups = "Group",   qc_types = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   num_cores = 1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: x Matrix data frame. Numeric data samples rows features columns. method Character. Transformation method apply. Options: "log2": Log base 2 transformation "log10": Log base 10 transformation "sqrt": Square root transformation "cbrt": Cube root transformation "clr": Centered log-ratio transformation (computed per sample) "arcsin_sqrt": Arcsine square root transformation (data must [0, 1] proportion-scale optional shift) "vsn": Variance Stabilizing Normalization (requires 'vsn' package) "glog": Generalized logarithm transformation (requires 'pmp' package) metadata Data frame. Sample metadata number rows equal nrow(x). Required glog transformation identify QC samples. Default: NULL. groups Character. Name column metadata containing group labels. Required glog transformation. Default: "Group". qc_types Character vector. Group labels identifying QC samples glog. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). num_cores Integer "max". Number CPU cores use VSN transformation. Use "max" automatic detection (uses available - 2). Default: 1. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: list containing: data Matrix data frame transformed data (class input x) method_used Character describing transformation method applied shift_applied Numeric value added data transformation (applicable) parameters List parameters used

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: Transformation Methods: log2 / log10: Logarithmic transformations compress large values expand small values. Useful data spanning multiple orders magnitude. data contains zeros negative values, shift applied first. sqrt: Square root transformation. Moderate variance stabilization. Works zero values requires non-negative data. cbrt: Cube root transformation. Can handle negative values natively; shift applied needed ensure consistency input range. Provides gentler compression log. clr: Centered log-ratio transformation. Maps compositional data simplex real space dividing value geometric mean row taking logarithm. Computed natively Base R efficiency. Suitable compositional relative-abundance data. data contains zeros negative values, shift applied first. arcsin_sqrt: Arcsine square root transformation. Variance-stabilizing transformation proportion data. Values must lie [0, 1] (non-negative rescalable [0, 1]). shift applied negative values; shifted data still contains values greater 1 function stops informative error, asin(sqrt(x > 1)) undefined reals. vsn: Variance Stabilizing Normalization. Model-based method microarray analysis calibrates variance across intensity range. Requires 'vsn' package. Data internally transposed calculation vsn expects features rows. Can computationally intensive; use num_cores parallel processing. glog: Generalized logarithm transformation. Hybrid log transformation handles low values better standard log. Requires 'pmp' package metadata QC sample labels. Use Method: log2/log10: common metabolomics. Use variance increases mean (heteroscedasticity). sqrt: variance proportional mean. clr: data compositional represent relative abundances. Preferred log-ratio methods require reference feature. arcsin_sqrt: data proportions bounded [0, 1]. Common relative abundance data ecology microbiomics. vsn: need sophisticated variance modeling. Recommended large datasets clear intensity-dependent variance. glog: Alternative log many low-intensity values. Good LC-MS data detection limits.

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: Aitchison, J. (1986). Statistical Analysis Compositional Data, Monographs Statistics Applied Probability. Chapman & Hall Ltd., London (UK). 416p. Huber, W., von Heydebreck, ., Sueltmann, H., Poustka, ., & Vingron, M. (2002). Variance stabilization applied microarray data calibration quantification differential expression. Bioinformatics, 18(Suppl 1), S96-S104. doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/18.suppl_1.s96 Parsons, H.M., Ludwig, C., Günther, U.L., & Viant, M.R. (2007). Improved classification accuracy 1- 2-dimensional NMR metabolomics data using variance stabilising generalised logarithm transformation. BMC Bioinformatics, 8, 234. doi:10.1186/1471-2105-8-234

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simulate data set.seed(123) x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100, sd = 50)),             nrow = 100, ncol = 50) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50)  # Log transformation result1 <- run_transform(x, method = "log2")  # CLR transformation (compositional data) result2 <- run_transform(x, method = "clr")  # Arcsine square root (proportion data in [0, 1]) x_prop <- x / rowSums(x) result3 <- run_transform(x_prop, method = "arcsin_sqrt")  # VSN with parallel processing result4 <- run_transform(x, method = "vsn", num_cores = 4)  # Glog transformation (requires metadata) metadata <- data.frame(   Sample = paste0("S", 1:100),   Group = rep(c("Control", "Treatment", "QC"), c(40, 40, 20)) ) result5 <- run_transform(x, method = "glog", metadata = metadata) } # }

what: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: Exports one data frames, tibbles, matrices single .xlsx file (.csv single-object exports).  x named list, tabular element written worksheet.  pondeR result objects (classes beginning run_) automatically unpacked extract tabular components. x contains direct tables holds list tables one level deep, nested list transparently unwrapped.

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
code: save_excel(   x,   filename = "output",   folder = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   freeze_first_row = TRUE,   freeze_first_col = FALSE,   auto_col_width = TRUE,   fd_date_stamp = FALSE,   fd_time_stamp = FALSE,   fn_date_stamp = FALSE,   fn_time_stamp = FALSE )

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: x data frame, tibble, matrix, pondeR result object (e.g. output run_auc(), run_diff()), named list whose elements combination types.  One level nesting automatically unpacked direct tabular elements found top level. filename single character string output file name.  Used -x single object.  Ignored x list multiple elements — element's name used sheet name list object's name becomes file name.  Illegal characters removed silently.  Defaults "output" supplied. folder single character string giving target directory. Supports nested folders (e.g., "Main folder/sub folder"). Created recursively absent. Defaults NULL (current working directory). overwrite Logical.  TRUE existing file silently overwritten.  FALSE (default) Windows-style counter suffix appended, e.g. "results (1).xlsx". freeze_first_row Logical.  TRUE (default) first row every worksheet frozen. freeze_first_col Logical.  TRUE first column every worksheet frozen.  Defaults FALSE. auto_col_width Logical.  TRUE (default) column widths auto-fitted. fd_date_stamp Logical.  Appends "MmmDDYYYY" date folder.  Default FALSE. fd_time_stamp Logical.  Appends "HHMM" time folder.  Default FALSE. fn_date_stamp Logical.  Appends date file name.  Default FALSE. fn_time_stamp Logical.  Appends time file name.  Default FALSE.

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: Called side effect.  Returns full normalised file path invisibly character string.

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: Sheet naming.  Sheet names taken names(x). Illegal Excel characters stripped, names truncated 25 characters (vowels removed end first, consonants needed), reserved name "History" becomes "History_", duplicates get " (N)" suffix (" (999)", hence 25-character base limit). Single vs. multi-object behaviour.  x single tabular object, supplied filename used exactly (sanitisation).  x list multiple elements, filename argument ignored element's name used sheet name; workbook file named list variable. pondeR result objects.  Objects whose class starts run_ (e.g. run_auc, run_diff) unpacked automatically tabular slots written separate sheets.

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: Exports single plot named list plots individual files. Supported plot types include ggplot2 objects, plotly widgets, grid grobs/gtables, recordedplot objects, zero-argument base-R drawing functions, pondeR result objects produced functions plot_diff(), run_auc(), plot_score(), plot_scree(), plot_volcano(). x single plot, filename used output file name.  x list plots, filename ignored element's list name used instead.  x contains direct plot objects top level holds list plots one level deep (e.g. result$plots), nested list transparently unpacked.

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
code: save_plots(   x,   filename = "plot",   format = NULL,   width = 15,   height = 12,   dpi = 300,   folder = NULL,   transparent = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE,   fd_date_stamp = FALSE,   fd_time_stamp = FALSE,   fn_date_stamp = FALSE,   fn_time_stamp = FALSE )

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: x single plot object named list plot objects.  Accepted types: ggplot, plotly, grob/gtable, recordedplot, zero-argument base-R drawing function, pondeR result object.  One level list nesting automatically unpacked direct plot objects found top level. filename single character string output file name.  Used x single plot.  x multi-plot list argument ignored element's name used file name. extension filename sets format unless format also supplied.  Defaults "plot" omitted. format Optional single character string overriding file extension exported files, e.g. "pdf", "svg", "png".  Case-insensitive; accepts without leading dot. supplied, extension filename ignored. width Numeric.  Output width inches.  Default 15. height Numeric.  Output height inches.  Default 12. dpi Numeric.  Resolution dots per inch raster formats. Default 300.  Ignored vector formats. folder single character string target directory. Supports nested folders (e.g., "Main folder/sub folder"). Created recursively absent. Defaults NULL (current working directory). transparent Logical.  Use transparent background.  Defaults FALSE.  warning issued white used fallback JPEG, support transparency. overwrite Logical.  Overwrite existing files TRUE. FALSE (default) Windows-style counter suffix appended, e.g. "plot (1).png". fd_date_stamp Logical.  Appends "MmmDDYYYY" date folder.  Default FALSE. fd_time_stamp Logical.  Appends "HHMM" time folder.  Default FALSE. fn_date_stamp Logical.  Appends date file name.  Default FALSE. fn_time_stamp Logical.  Appends time file name.  Default FALSE.

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: Called side effect.  Returns full normalised file path(s) saved plot(s) invisibly character vector.

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: Single vs. multi-plot behaviour.  single plot object uses filename exactly (sanitisation).  list multiple elements ignores filename; element's list name becomes file name.  Illegal characters stripped, duplicate names resolved (1), (2), etc. Unnamed list elements.  Elements without name receive placeholder name "plot_N" N position. pondeR result objects.  Objects whose class starts "plot_" matches known pondeR class unpacked automatically. first extractable plot exported; note printed multiple plots present. plotly HTML export.  Requires htmlwidgets.  Static raster export plotly objects requires plotly working reticulate/kaleido installation: reticulate::py_install("kaleido").

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2)  # Single ggplot — filename is respected p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() save_plots(p, filename = "AUROC All (Neg)")  # List of ggplots — filename is ignored; list names are used p2 <- ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, colour = Species)) +         geom_point() save_plots(list(scatter = p, iris_plot = p2))  # Accessing a list slot with multiple plots (auto-unpacked) res <- run_auc(data = mydata, response = "group", predictors = c("A","B")) save_plots(res$plots, folder = "figures")  # Override format for all plots save_plots(list(scatter = p, iris_plot = p2), format = "pdf")  # Base-R plot via a zero-argument function save_plots(list(hist_mpg = function() hist(mtcars$mpg, main = "MPG"))) } # }
